title: AS-signed session authority: sessions signed by AS, node verifies offline; self-hosted + cloud split model
description: Move session-token signing from CP to AS (offline-verified by nodes via pinned AS pubkey) so a compromised CP can't forge session authority. Self-hosted: AS-signed proof of account O, node checks O==accountId. Cloud: AS attests identity / CP supplies routing / node enforces ownership+tenancy. See sp-ova §7a.
close_reason: AS session-token MECHANISM done (commit 6b34091): internal/sessiontoken Ed25519 Sign/Verify (offline; rejects tamper/wrong-key/expiry) + authsvc session key/IssueSessionToken/SessionPubKey + /session/pubkey. Live-flow consumption (CP issue on session-open, node verify + self-hosted/cloud split on attach) = follow-up sp-rjr (spans WS + node relay + web, overlaps E4). TDD green; build/vet/fmt clean.

title: Tenancy gate: self-hosted single-tenant (owner==node accountId), cloud multi-tenant
description: CP placement: a spawn owned by O may go to any cloud node OR a self-hosted node whose verified SAN accountId==O; NEVER place O's spawn on a self-hosted node bound to O'!=O. Node also refuses to run a spawn whose owner != its accountId. Review status is NOT a trust boundary (apps run anywhere). See sp-ova §6.
close_reason: Tenancy gate done (commit ec77df3): registry.PickFor enforces cloud=multi-tenant / self-hosted=owner-only; placementFor returns {Owner} for all tiers (review tier only gates unverified-author authorization). Fixes latent bug (reviewed app could land on another owner's self-hosted node). Node-side defense-in-depth refusal -> sp-1g3 (needs node cert identity). TDD TestPickForTenancy; full repo 28 green.

title: CP node-auth enforcement + insecure/enforced modes + drop self-asserted Register fields
description: Enforced mode: mTLS listener for NodeService pinned to the Root CA; derive {nodeId,accountId,class} from the verified peer-cert SAN in runNode (NEVER from Register). Insecure mode: current plaintext h2c on a dev port for unit/e2e/local. Remove self-asserted node_class/node_owner from proto/node + runNode. CP holds only public pins. See sp-ova §5/§8/§10.
notes: PROGRESS (7826958): nodeauth core landed (internal/cp/nodeauth) — DeriveIdentity (cert->identity, rejects no-cert + forged-cloud), WithIdentity/IdentityFromContext, Middleware(insecure|enforced). TDD green. REMAINING: (1) runNode prefers ctx identity over self-asserted Register (insecure keeps Register fallback so existing tests pass); (2) cmd/cp main.go enforced-mode mTLS listener (ClientAuth: RequireAnyClientCert + middleware verify against pinned root) wrapping the NodeService handler, insecure=current h2c; (3) node-side presents cert (-> sp-1g3).
close_reason: CP enforcement done (commit fd2a12a): nodeauth core + runNode takes id from verified cert (ignores self-asserted Register when authenticated; insecure keeps fallback) + cmd/cp NODE_AUTH_MODE insecure(h2c, default)/enforced(dedicated mTLS listener, RequireAnyClientCert + middleware verify vs pinned root). Full proto-field REMOVAL deferred until enforced-only (would break node sender + insecure tests); fields are ignored-when-authenticated for now. TDD green (TestRegisterPrefersVerifiedIdentity); full repo suite 28 pkgs green.

title: Node enrollment via AS: owner-authenticated one-time token, node keypair+CSR, AS issues self-hosted cert
description: AS authenticates the requesting owner (it is the IdP) and issues a one-time, short-lived, single-use, account-scoped enrollment token bound to that accountId. Node generates its own keypair (private key never leaves the box), submits CSR+token; AS verifies and issues a self-hosted cert with SAN <nodeId>.<accountId>.self-hosted.nodes.spawnery.internal from the held intermediate. See sp-ova §3/§4.
close_reason: Enrollment vertical done (commit 563dd42): pki NewNodeCSR/SignCSR (proof-of-possession; CA-chosen SAN) + CSR PEM; authsvc IssueEnrollmentToken (one-time, account-bound, ~10m, atomic consume), Enroll, POST /enroll, RunEnroll node client. Cert bound to token's account + node's own key, class always self-hosted. Owner OAuth gating = E4 (out of scope; token endpoint not exposed unauthenticated). TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

title: Auth Service skeleton: own container, holds self-hosted intermediate key, publishes verification pubkeys
description: New AS service (separate container/node) = identity root of trust. Holds the self-hosted intermediate private key (never exported) + the AS session-signing key. Health/config; insecure vs enforced mode; publishes its verification pubkeys (Root CA pin distribution, AS session pubkey). No issuance logic yet. See sp-ova §1/§10.
close_reason: AS skeleton done (worktree branch, commit 972fac2): internal/authsvc Service holds the self-hosted intermediate + issues self-hosted-only certs (verify against published root), Load from PEM, HTTP Handler (/healthz + /ca/root export), cmd/authsvc container entrypoint w/ graceful shutdown. Enrollment-token auth = sp-0qc, AS-signed sessions = sp-3ca build on top. TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

title: PKI/CA tooling + fixtures: Root CA, name-constrained cloud+self-hosted intermediates, per-env root, cloud leaf mint, hardcoded test Root CA
description: Foundation. Offline ceremony tooling to generate a Root CA and two intermediates: cloud (offline/operator) and self-hosted (name-constrained to self-hosted.nodes.spawnery.internal, later held by the AS). Cloud-leaf minting tool. Per-environment Root CA (no shared CA). A hardcoded TEST Root CA + cert fixtures for enforced-mode tests. Go crypto/x509 name-constraint create+verify; class encoded in DNS SAN. See sp-ova design §4/§8.
close_reason: pki library complete + tested (commit b941061 on worktree branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova): Root CA, name-constrained cloud/self-hosted intermediates (self-hosted cryptographically can't forge cloud — proven load-bearing), IssueNode w/ SAN identity, Verify (chain+constraints+expiry)->Identity, PEM persistence, TLSCertificate. Offline-mint CLI + checked-in test-Root-CA fixture deferred to consumers (T9 e2e builds its own test CA via the API; a thin cmd/spawnery-ca wrapper can be added if/when ops needs it).

title: Implement node auth + Auth Service (sp-ova): unified identity, PKI, tenancy, AS sessions
description: Implementation of the sp-ova design (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-node-auth-unified-identity-design.md). Separate Auth Service as identity root of trust; CP holds only public pins; CA-cert node identity (SAN-encoded class via name-constrained intermediates, cloud intermediate offline); cloud multi-tenant / self-hosted single-tenant tenancy gate; AS-signed offline-verified sessions; insecure vs enforced modes with PKI-soundness e2e tests; per-env Root CA. Children are dependency-sequenced. Confidentiality (E2E relay sp-4n9/sp-zdd) and web-SPA-delivery (sp-ore) are tracked separately.
close_reason: All 9 children closed. Node-auth + Auth Service implemented end-to-end TDD on branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova (9 commits b941061..3f56186): pki name-constrained CA, authsvc (enrollment + AS sessions), CP cert-derived identity + insecure/enforced modes + mTLS listener, tenancy gate, node mTLS dial, sessiontoken, clientverify, PKI-soundness e2e. Full repo 31 pkgs green; golangci-lint 0 issues; insecure default keeps dev/tests unchanged. title: [SECURITY/ARCH] gVisor breaks the shared-netns pod: agent can't reach sidecar on 127.0.0.1 under runsc
description:
LOCAL-VERIFIED (this host, runsc release-20260525.0). A real goose+sidecar spawn was driven under both runtimes via standalone spawnlet + spawnctl 'what is the secret word?':
- runc: goose reached the sidecar (127.0.0.1:8080), full ACP round-trip, real LLM reply ('The secret word is QUOKKA-4417'). WORKS.
- runsc: goose BOOTED + ACP-initialized + accepted the prompt (so goose IS gVisor-compatible — no syscall/ENOSYS issues), but failed with 'Network error: Could not connect to 127.0.0.1:8080'. The SIDECAR IS UNREACHABLE.
ROOT CAUSE: our pod is two Docker containers sharing a netns (agent started with --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches the sidecar's inference proxy on loopback). Under per-container gVisor, EACH container is its own Sentry with its OWN user-space netstack — Docker's container:<id> netns-sharing does NOT bridge the two Sentries' loopbacks, so the agent's 127.0.0.1 is not the sidecar's. The gVisor research flagged this: k8s puts a WHOLE POD in ONE runsc sandbox; Docker 'docker run' per-container does not.
=> gVisor adoption (sp-7k8) CANNOT use the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod. Options to design: (a) run the pod as ONE runsc sandbox (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass — whole pod = one Sentry, shared loopback, the documented model); (b) collapse the sidecar into the agent container (single container = agent + inference-proxy process, one sandbox, loopback works); (c) separate netns + agent reaches sidecar via bridge IP:8080 (weakens isolation, cross-container exposure, and conflicts with the RFC1918 egress drop — messy). DESIGN-FIRST. This is a HARD prerequisite for Phase-2 gVisor on cloud; verified that goose, hardening (cap-drop), preflight, and the host egress floor all work under runsc — only the inter-container loopback breaks.
notes:
DECISION (user 2026-06-01): for RUNSC use a one-sandbox pod via containerd CRI (RunPodSandbox handler=runsc + 2 containers: sidecar+agent, separate mount/pid ns sharing the pod netns -> loopback works, KEY STAYS ISOLATED in the sidecar). For RUNC keep the current Docker two-container shared-netns approach unchanged. FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED on this host: containerd 1.7.24 CRI plugin enabled+ok, /run/containerd/containerd.sock live, containerd-shim-runsc-v1 present. STILL NEEDED: (1) containerd config — add a [plugins...cri.containerd.runtimes.runsc] handler (runtime_type io.containerd.runsc.v1) + restart; (2) CNI for pod-sandbox networking (cri plugin needs a CNI bridge plugin/config). EPIC SCOPE (large): node gains a CRI pod backend (k8s.io/cri-api gRPC client to the containerd sock) selected when CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc; manager.Create branches runc(Docker two-container) vs runsc(CRI pod); sub-systems: CRI RunPodSandbox/CreateContainer/StartContainer x2, CRI ImageService pull (containerd's k8s.io image namespace is SEPARATE from Docker's — images must be pulled there), stdio Attach to the agent container over CRI streaming for the ACP bridge, egress floor on the POD IP but the CNI bridge is not docker0 so DOCKER-USER may not be in-path -> floor likely needs a raw-FORWARD/custom host chain for CRI pods, RemovePodSandbox teardown, runsc preflight under CRI. Cleanest seam: a runtime.Pod abstraction both backends implement (Docker=2 containers shared netns; CRI=pod). DESIGN-FIRST.
RESOLVED + REAL-HOST VERIFIED (runsc release-20260525.0, this host). The one-sandbox CRI pod (handler=runsc, sidecar+agent sharing the pod netns) fixes agent<->sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080 — the original sp-vaw break. BUT the runsc round-trip needed two further fixes found running E2E_TEST_RUNSC.md: (1) node<->agent ACP transport — gVisor isolates the in-sandbox abstract-UDS namespace from the host, so the runc-lane setns+UDS attach can't reach the adapter; switched the CRI lane to TCP on the pod IP (adapter listens tcp://0.0.0.0:7000, node dials podIP:7000). (2) POD_DNS — kubelet-less CRI pods inherited the 127.0.0.53 stub resolver so the sidecar couldn't resolve the model upstream. With these: full goose round-trip 'QUOKKA-4417', 5/5 create->teardown cycles with zero leaks, per-pod SPAWNLET-EGRESS floor enforced (metadata+RFC1918 dropped in-agent, public+DNS ok). Fixes committed (b4e1b4b). docker-29 ContainerIP compat also fixed in the same commit.

title: [SECURITY] CP must authenticate nodes + verify cloud-class — today NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are self-asserted on an UNAUTHENTICATED stream (cloud-spoofing)
description:
CRITICAL TRUST GAP. The node<->CP Attach stream has NO auth (cmd/cp/main.go:103 'node side: no auth'); a node self-reports node_class + node_owner in its Register message (sp-2as/sp-t5p). So ANY node that can reach the CP can claim NODE_CLASS=cloud (and any node_owner) and be treated as a trusted, Spawnery-operated cloud node. Combined with class/owner-based routing (sp-t5p) and any future cross-user/secret data flow (vended storage tokens, other users' spawns, audit data), a malicious self-hosted node could SPOOF a cloud spawnlet and receive other users' secret data. The cloud/self-hosted trust split (sp-rpa floor enforcement, sp-t5p routing) currently RESTS ON unauthenticated self-assertion — only safe today because the demo runs solely Spawnery-operated nodes on a closed CP.

NOTE: there is NO node keypair / secret-passing crypto in the codebase yet (only crypto/tls transport + crypto/rand for ids). The 'spawnlets generate pubkeys for secret passing' is E0/E4 DESIGN, deferred in the demo cut — not built. So this must be designed, not just wired.

PROPOSED (user 2026-06-01): cloud spawnlets present a node identity cert whose pubkey is signed by Spawnery's ROOT CA; CP PINS that root and verifies the chain on Attach before honoring class=cloud or routing any cross-user/secret data. Self-hosted nodes are unauthenticated/lower-trust and may only run their OWN owner's spawns (aligns with sp-t5p: unverified->author's-own-node; a self-hosted node should be bound to a single authenticated owner, and CP must verify node_owner == the connecting identity, not trust the self-reported value). Likely: per-node keypair generated on the node; CSR/enrollment signed by Spawnery CA for cloud nodes; mTLS or signed-Register-token on the Attach stream; CP rejects class=cloud without a valid Spawnery-CA-chained cert; CP derives node_owner from the authenticated identity for self-hosted, not from the self-reported field. Interacts with E4 identity/secrets (sp-7h6) and the sp-gtm CP-key-vending MITM finding. DESIGN-FIRST. Until landed: document that NODE_CLASS/NODE_OWNER are trusted only in a closed all-Spawnery-operated deployment.
notes:
DESIGN DONE (2026-06-05): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-node-auth-unified-identity-design.md (v2). Key decisions: separate Auth Service (own container) is the identity root of trust; CP holds NO signing keys, only public pins; node identity = CA cert with SAN <nodeId>.<accountId>.<class>.nodes.spawnery.internal (no class/owner payload claim); class unforgeable via name-constrained intermediates (cloud intermediate OFFLINE, self-hosted held by AS); tenancy = cloud multi-tenant / self-hosted single-tenant (review status is NOT a trust boundary); AS-signed sessions; insecure vs enforced modes (no migration, pre-demo); per-env Root CA. Surfaced + filed: sp-4n9 (E2E relay encryption, under new epic sp-zdd), sp-ore (web SPA off the CP). Implementation decomposed under epic <EPIC_ID>.
(Implementation epic = sp-g4w.)
IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-06-05) on branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova via epic sp-g4w (all 9 tasks closed). The spoof is closed by running NODE_AUTH_MODE=enforced (mTLS, cert-derived identity, tenancy gate). insecure remains the default so nothing changes until the enforced flip + staging/prod CA exist. Remaining before this bug is fully retired in prod: deploy enforced mode + per-env Root CA; full proto-field removal; live session-flow wiring (sp-rjr); cert vending to clients (sp-gtm/sp-zdd). Leaving sp-ova open for the deployment decision.

title: [bug] egress floor is in-pod-netns iptables — gVisor netstack only partially supports iptables; move floor to host veth
description: HOST-VERIFICATION RESEARCH (gvisor.md §4): our sp-rpa egress floor applies iptables INSIDE the container netns via nsenter. Under runc that's the real kernel netns (verified working). Under gVisor (runsc, netstack), the pod's netns is the Sentry's user-space TCP/IP stack which only PARTIALLY supports iptables — our DROP rules may silently behave differently / not enforce. Since we plan to adopt gVisor for the cloud tier, the floor must move OUT of the pod netns to the HOST: enforce on the host veth ingress hook / DOCKER-USER chain / CNI, where the real Linux netfilter sees gVisor packets exiting the veth normally. Affects firewall.NsenterApplier placement. Pre-req for gVisor adoption; also more robust under runc. Re-verify metadata+RFC1918 drop from the host side.
notes: EMPIRICALLY PROVEN on this host (runsc release-20260525.0). IN-NETNS floor (current NsenterApplier) under runsc = COMPLETE NO-OP: host-netns iptables DROP counters all 0 pkts, RFC1918 10.0.0.1 REACHABLE (vs blocked + 3-pkt counters under runc). gVisor netstack handles TCP/IP in user space + emits raw frames on the veth, so host-kernel netfilter in the container netns never sees workload traffic. => flipping on runsc TODAY would silently make the egress floor a no-op (metadata/RFC1918/exfil all open). HARD gVisor pre-req, P0. FIX VALIDATED: host DOCKER-USER rules matched by the pod's bridge source IP enforce under BOTH runtimes — under runsc, -s <containerIP> -d 169.254.0.0/16/-d RFC1918 DROP matched 3 pkts each, public reachable, dns :53 ACCEPT present. IMPL: get the SIDECAR container's bridge IP (docker inspect NetworkSettings.IPAddress — the netns owner; agent shares it via container:netns so no own IP); insert ordered DOCKER-USER rules (-s ip): udp/tcp 53 ACCEPT + EgressAllowCIDRs, then 169.254/16 + RFC1918 DROPs (insert reversed so final order is accepts-before-drops, or use a per-spawn child chain). NEW LIFECYCLE: DOCKER-USER persists across containers (unlike the auto-cleaned netns) — manager.Stop MUST remove this spawn's rules; track the IP per spawn. loopback (agent<->sidecar 127.0.0.1) is never forwarded so the host floor doesn't touch it. 172.16/12 drop is fine (blocks cross-container/internal; egress dest is external/public, allowed).
close_reason: Done+host-verified, merged b9f5342: egress floor moved from in-netns nsenter (PROVEN no-op under gVisor/runsc) to host DOCKER-USER chain matched by pod bridge IP (firewall.HostFloorApplier + Rules(ip); ContainerIP). Apply-on-create fail-closed, REMOVE-on-stop (FloorIP tracked). Shipped egress_e2e PASSES as root (metadata+RFC1918 dropped, public reachable) under runc AND validated equivalent under runsc. Unblocks gVisor adoption.

title: DEMO: automated App-review scanner is a committed demo build item
description: Open third-party publishing was chosen for the demo, so the E8 §5 LLM-as-judge App-review scanner (prompt-injection / data-exfiltration / jailbreak / scope-sanity over manifest+persona+skills) is NO LONGER deferred -- it gates every open publish and is the thing that makes open publishing safe. Real ML/eval build item. Run it OFF the DeepSeek inference GPU (sp-iui). Tune against curated red-team prompts + the seed apps. Affects E8 §5, E5 open-registry gate.
notes: REFRAMED via trust tiers (user decision): apps carry a per-version trust tier — unverified (published, not scanned) / scanned (passed this automated scanner) / reviewed (human). Runtime restrictions + user-facing warnings SCALE with tier; the enforced sandbox floor (egress + isolation + per-app consent) applies to ALL tiers. So the scanner is the 'scanned' tier mechanism, NOT a perfect single gate — a scanner miss is contained by the floor + the tighter unverified-tier sandbox + informed-consent disclosure. This de-risks the scanner's load-bearingness. Tier drops to 'scanned' on new version; permission escalation drops 'reviewed'. Seed apps ship 'reviewed'. Spec: E5 §5, E8 §5, scope §4.

title: DEMO-ROAST: threat-model inversion — user-steered agent IS the untrusted vector
description: The demo deferred egress enforcement (sp-ba5) and chose PLAIN CONTAINERS (no gVisor/microVM), both justified by 'no arbitrary creator code; Spawnery-vetted runtime only'. FALSE for the demo: the spawn runs a coding-style agent with shell/file/network tools, steered turn-by-turn by an untrusted free-tier stranger. The USER is the untrusted-code path. Consequences: open SSRF/egress-proxy/metadata-cred-theft by design (no egress allowlist), and container-escape/compute-abuse surface (plain containers + shell-capable agent). The security machinery cut for B+Y was load-bearing for B+Y too. FIX: treat the demo's spawn as hostile; pull egress floor (D2) + isolation hardening + resource limits (D3) + restricted toolset (D6) into the demo. Affects E1 isolation decision, E0/E8 egress, demo-mvp-scope.
notes: USER DECISION 2026-06-01: restricted agent toolset is POST-MVP/backlog. Strategy = allow-all tools by default; rely on REVIEW/AUDIT mechanisms (app-review scanner sp-5a9 + E8 audit) to detect abuse; escalate to VM/microVM isolation in the CLOUD if the platform takes off (not gVisor-per-spawn now). The OTHER sp-eha pieces ARE shipped: egress floor (sp-rpa), per-spawn cgroup limits + per-user quota + optional gVisor runtime knob (sp-ach). So sp-eha's hardening floor is substantially in place; only the toolset-restriction sub-item is deferred. Abuse-control for the demo now leans on sp-5a9 (scanner) + egress+limits floor + trust tiers + informed consent.

title: DEMO-ROAST: ship a hard egress allowlist floor in the demo (not post-MVP)
description: Egress enforcement was deferred as 'permissions/consent' (sp-ba5), but the per-spawn EGRESS FLOOR (a hard default, not per-app consent UI) is cheap and closes most network abuse. FIX: per-spawn network policy blocking everything except the localhost model sidecar + GitHub API/host + any app-declared domains; explicitly DROP 169.254.169.254 (cloud metadata) + RFC1918 internal ranges. Owner = E1 (pod netns). This is the single highest value/cost fix in the demo. Closes SSRF, egress-proxy, metadata-cred-theft, arbitrary-host exfil.
notes: CONFIRMED demo-required: egress floor is mandatory for the open marketplace.
close_reason: Egress block-floor implemented + merged 1435585: firewall pkg (Rules block-floor: drop 169.254/16 + RFC1918, allow lo + operator CIDRs, default-allow public; NsenterApplier), runtime.ContainerPID, manager.Create applies fail-closed BEFORE agent starts, node-class aware (cloud always enforces non-disableable; self-hosted honors EgressEnforce; default cloud). Hermetic tests (rule ordering, fail-closed, cloud-forces, self-hosted-opt-out) green; final review APPROVE. CAVEAT: real packet-drop UNVERIFIED in dev sandbox (no iptables/root) — build-tagged egress_e2e must run on node host (see sp-* follow-up).

title: ROAST: privacy/brand claims dishonest as written — reframe
description: Found by 2/4 (security #2/#3/#7, product F1-F5/F7). Multiple headline claims are weasel-worded for the cloud/free path: (a) 'E2E / CP never sees content' — channel terminates at the NODE, which on cloud IS Spawnery and audits content; (b) 'BYO key never reaches us' — on cloud nodes the node decrypts the plaintext BYO key + audits content; (c) 'your data stays yours' — CP holds GitHub-App signer + can mint write tokens to your repo silently; (d) 'one-click' — see ROAST-6. Reframe all copy: 'encrypted in transit to the Spawnery-operated node that runs your agent; on cloud, content is audited for abuse.' Reserve absolute privacy claims for self-host only. Affects system-design §1/§6/§7/§9.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP is total SPOF + relay bandwidth cliff
description: Found by ops #1/#2. The single central CP relays ALL non-LAN-direct session ciphertext (every token, every turn) AND mints tokens AND schedules. A CP restart/deploy/OOM drops every active conversation simultaneously and blocks new sessions = 100% cloud blast radius. No HA/drain/reconnect story. Relaying all conversation bytes = 2x egress through one box, saturates on bandwidth + connection count long before compute. FIX: split a stateless horizontally-scaled relay dataplane from control logic; session-resumption tokens (reconnect without re-auth); blue/green relay; prioritize LAN-direct/BYO-ingress off the post-MVP shelf; publish SLO + client reconnect behavior. Affects E0 §1/§10, E1.

title: ROAST: BYO-on-cloud audit has no inspection point — mechanical gap
description: Found by 2/4 (arch #1, security #3). Four docs assert 'BYO-on-cloud is still audited' but NO component sees BYO model traffic: the sidecar calls the provider directly (E2 §1), the node only terminates the transport channel + forwards ACP frames over loopback (not model prompts/responses), and the E8 classifier is scoped to 'Spawnery-operated inference' which BYO bypasses. FIX: make the SIDECAR the single audit/classifier point (it's the only component seeing all model traffic, managed + BYO; it's a Spawnery-controlled container). Reconcile every 'node is the audit point' phrasing to the sidecar. Affects E2 §1/§4, E8 §1/§2, E0 §9/§10, system §3.

title: ROAST: home-machine singleton + no DR/failover
description: Found by ops #3/#4. (a) One physical box runs the whole free tier + DeepSeek on one GPU; realistic ceiling ~5-20 concurrent generations; if it dies the free tier is hard-down (burst triggers on OVERLOAD from a live heartbeat, not on a DEAD node). (b) No backup/DR for CP state (spawn index, catalog, GitHub App signing JWT, OAuth refresh tokens, vault wraps, audit store) — DB loss = users can't find spawns, must re-auth all storage, vault ciphertext gone. FIX: active/standby or dead-node->cloud failover (not just overload->burst); decouple inference GPU pool from container CPU pool; CP DB backup/PITR/geo-replication; GitHub App key + OAuth secrets in KMS not the DB; document what's reconstructable from spawn.yml. Affects system §1, E1 §5, E0.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

title: ROAST: CP key-vending enables trivial MITM — 'E2E' unverifiable
description: Found by 3/4 reviewers (security #1/#2, ops #10, product F3). The client receives the node static pubkey FROM the CP with no out-of-band verification (deferred post-MVP). A malicious/compromised CP substitutes its own key, runs key agreement as the client, decrypts BYO keys + full conversation, re-encrypts to the real node. Classic unauthenticated-DH key substitution. Worse: Spawnery is simultaneously CA + relay + key-directory, so even cert pinning doesn't stop the operator minting a node cert. The 'CP relays only opaque ciphertext / never sees content' claim is structurally false against the operator. FIX: vend full CA-signed cert chain + pin Spawnery CA root in the client bundle (not fetched at runtime); make out-of-band/TOFU node-key pinning MVP (esp. self-host); add key-transparency log. Affects E0 §10, E4 §6/§8, E6 §2.

title: Cross-component APIs & contracts
description: The shared seams every component depends on. Defines: spawneryapp.yml manifest schema; spawn.yml schema (data-repo source of truth, secrets excluded); container orchestration interface (start/exec/stop); ACP client<->agent orchestration contract; storage-adapter interface (materialize/persist); model-sidecar OpenAI-compatible API + central-gateway protocol + metering/audit hooks; CP index API; edge-activator session-token/auth protocol; permission/consent + egress-allowlist schema; catalog/registry index API. Design-first; all other epics consume these contracts. See design doc sections 2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10.
notes:
Design doc (v1, interview complete): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-spawnery-e0-contracts-design.md — see Appendix decision log (E0.1–E0.9).
v2 corrections: CP = single central managed instance (self-host = node agents attaching to it); rendezvous relay in CP; s2s=gRPC; Apps agent-agnostic (agent chosen at spawn, manifest declares compat); skills imported via agent's native process; storage detail -> E3; personalization + permissions -> post-MVP (TODO.md). Amended system-design §1/§4/§10.

title: Runtime & orchestration core
description: Control plane, node agent, container lifecycle (ephemeral, scale-to-zero), ACP orchestration layer, pluggable isolation-backend abstraction (concrete impls deferred), scheduler/placement (local-first, audit-flag set at placement). Design doc sections 3,4.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-spawnery-e1-runtime-core-design.md. Decisions: per-agent base image + mount definition (no per-(App,agent) build); cold-start every wake (warm pools post-MVP); placement = open app + owner node -> owner node else cloud; 2-D burst (CPU->offload containers, GPU->offload inference); idle/close/cap teardown, resume from /data; pod = base[agent+bridge+toolset]+sidecar; Docker/Podman backend first. Refined E0 §7/§3.

title: ACP enrich task 0: Frame envelope foundation (typed-payload hybrid)
description: Introduce the typed-payload hybrid Frame: keep Seq+Kind envelope and existing scalar fields for current kinds; add optional nested payload pointers (Tool*, Plan[], Usage*, Reason, Error*, Cmds[], Mode*, Options[], OptionID) for rich/new kinds. Go internal/node/frame.go + TS web/src/acp/frames.ts with discriminated-union types keyed on kind. All existing kinds stay green & byte-stable. Round-trip encode/decode tests. Foundation — blocks all other tasks. Files: internal/node/frame.go, web/src/acp/frames.ts.
close_reason: Frame envelope foundation complete: typed-payload hybrid (Go frame.go + TS frames.ts), all existing kinds byte-stable, round-trip+byte-stability tests, encodeFrame marshal-error hardened. Spec ✅ + code-quality ✅. On branch worktree-acp-enrichment (3cc7f6e..abfc22a).

description: Hardcoded, shared (CP+node) registry mapping binary -> [runnable]. Runnable = {id, mode(acp|tmux|served), launch argv, resume argv, relay path, label}. Seed: goose->{goose-acp,goose-tui}, opencode->{opencode-served}, claude-code->{claude-tui}.

title: Tmux terminal mode + agent container capability taxonomy
description: Add a side-by-side 'tmux' run mode (any TUI agent in tmux, xterm.js web + spawnctl attach to one shared session) alongside existing acp/served modes, driven by an image -> binary -> runnable capability taxonomy. Spawnery derives web rendering + spawnctl behavior per spawn from the runnable's mode. See spec for full design, modes->surfaces matrix, and phasing.

title: Wire AS-signed session tokens into the live WS session-open path (node verify + tenancy split)
description: sp-3ca built the AS session-token mechanism (internal/sessiontoken + authsvc IssueSessionToken/SessionPubKey). REMAINING integration: CP requests an AS-signed session token on session open (POST /spawns/{id}/session), the client presents it, and the NODE verifies it offline against the pinned AS pubkey before relaying — applying the split model (self-hosted: token owner == node accountId; cloud: AS identity + CP routing + node enforce). Touches the WS/session flow (internal/cp/ws.go), the node relay (internal/node), and the web client. Overlaps E4 sec7.

title: PKI-soundness e2e suite: enforced mode vs test Root CA, negative cases (wrong class/tenant/chain/expiry/forged session)
description: A dedicated enforced-mode e2e test class against the hardcoded test Root CA that asserts enforcement REJECTS: a self-hosted intermediate signing a cloud-subtree leaf; placing O's spawn on a O'-bound self-hosted node; an unchained/self-signed cert; an expired cert; a forged (non-AS) session token. Proves the PKI is sound. See sp-ova §8.
close_reason: PKI-soundness e2e done (commit 3f56186): real mTLS handshake vs enforced middleware accepts valid self-hosted+cloud, rejects forged-cloud/expired/wrong-root/no-cert; forged session token rejected; clientverify host-binding. Full repo 31 pkgs green; golangci-lint 0 issues.

title: Client-side infra verification (node + spawnctl): pin Root CA + AS keys, verify hosting node cert+SAN before trust
description: Native clients bake in the pinned Root CA + AS verification keys and verify the hosting node's cert chains to the Root with SAN accountId==mine (self-hosted) or class==cloud (multi-tenant) BEFORE trusting it / encrypting to it. Detects a compromised CP rerouting workloads. (Web client = separate sp-ore.) See sp-ova §7.
close_reason: Client verification PRIMITIVE done (commit f8c6339): clientverify.VerifyHost checks host node cert chains to pinned root + SAN matches expectation (self-hosted==my account / cloud multi-tenant). TDD covers the §7 attacks (foreign self-hosted, forged cloud, class mismatch). Consume-side wiring (spawnctl/node call it on a CP-vended node cert) depends on cert-vending = sp-gtm/sp-zdd; web delivery = sp-ore. Build/vet/fmt clean.

title: Node mTLS dial + cert storage (spawnlet presents AS-issued cert on Attach; enforced/insecure)
description: Spawnlet stores its enrollment cert and dials the CP over mTLS in enforced mode (presenting the cert); insecure mode keeps the plaintext dev path. See sp-ova §3.3/§5.
close_reason: Node mTLS done (commit 8c7992b): internal/node/nodeid (Save/Load identity, MTLSClient = h2 client w/ node cert + pinned CP root); cmd/spawnlet nodeCPClient picks transport by NODE_AUTH_MODE (insecure=h2c/CP_ADDR; enforced=load-or-enroll identity, mTLS to CP_NODE_ADDR). Node-side tenancy refusal needs spawn-owner in StartSpawn (contract change) -> deferred; CP gate authoritative. TDD green; full repo 29.

title: Serve the web SPA independently of the CP (CDN/static origin) + pin Root CA & AS keys in the bundle
description: Node-auth design finding (sp-ova §7): if the CP serves the web client, a COMPROMISED CP ships JS that fakes every client-side verification — making web-side compromise detection theater. Move SPA delivery to an origin NOT controlled by the CP (basic CDN / static host with subresource integrity or signed releases), and bake the pinned Root CA + AS verification pubkeys into the bundle (not fetched from the CP at runtime). Native clients (node, spawnctl) are immune via baked-in pins; this closes the web-client gap. Prereq for the sp-ova §7 confidentiality guarantee to hold for web users.

title: E2E-encrypt agent relay traffic (client↔node) so the CP relays only ciphertext
description: TODAY the per-spawn relay ships PLAINTEXT frames through the CP (internal/node pump <-> CP <-> client), so a compromised CP reads all agent conversation + any BYO secrets regardless of node identity. Implement the E0 §10 end-to-end channel: client and node establish a confidential channel keyed to the node's VERIFIED identity (cert from the AS root per sp-ova), CP relays opaque ciphertext only. Surfaced as the gating residual in the node-auth design (sp-ova §7): the AS split makes node-key vending verifiable but does NOT by itself encrypt the relay — this task is the confidentiality half. Pairs with sp-gtm (verifiable key delivery).

title: E2E encryption — client↔node confidential channel + verifiable key delivery
description: Umbrella for end-to-end confidentiality of agent traffic and BYO secrets: the CP must relay only ciphertext it cannot read, and clients must be able to VERIFY the node key they encrypt to (not trust the CP's word). Spans the E0 §10 E2E channel contract, the relay-encryption mechanism, and verifiable/pinned key delivery. The node-auth design (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-node-auth-unified-identity-design.md, sp-ova) provides the AS-anchored, client-verifiable node identity these depend on; this epic delivers the confidentiality half. Closely tied to sp-gtm (key-vending MITM).

title: Suspend/resume: capture opencode SQLite state dir + quiesce/checkpoint before snapshot
description: opencode persists session state in SQLite. (1) Verify opencode's data dir (may default to $HOME/.local/share/opencode or a project dir) is INSIDE a dirty-tree-captured mount; relocate via env/config if not, else resume loses history. (2) Quiesce: SQLite+WAL is written continuously; stop/quiesce opencode or checkpoint the WAL before the dirty-tree capture so the restored DB is consistent (no torn snapshot). On resume the adapter discovers the existing session.

title: Node neutralization: agent-neutral ACP cleanup + episode-end signal + conformance test
description: Node already speaks REAL spec-ACP (internal/acp; pickPermOption keys on ACP option kind). (1) Rename misleading 'goose' comments/field docs in internal/node/pump.go to agent-neutral. (2) Verify pickPermOption against the exact ACP kinds the opencode adapter emits. (3) Episode-end signal: opencode serve is long-lived and never exits per-session, so the old 'spawn over when agent exits' signal is gone — verify the lifecycle FSM drives spawn lifetime from CP stop/suspend + idle reaper, not agent-process exit. (4) Add a small canonical-ACP conformance test fixture.
notes: DONE (partial): comments neutralized goose->agent in pump.go; canonical-ACP conformance test added (updateToFrame + pickPermOption on spec kinds) — proves node is agent-neutral. REMAINING: episode-end FSM (folded into sp-5h3.4, the TCP-unification task): with the long-lived adapter, a dropped node<->adapter connection is reconnectable, not spawn-over; verify exitFn/lifecycle no longer treats agent-conn EOF as episode end.

title: Unified TCP ACP transport: node dials TCP for both lanes; remove docker stdio path
description: Both runc (docker shared-netns) and runsc (CRI) lanes use the same TCP ACP socket the runsc lane already uses. Remove the docker-API stdio-attach code path and the abstract-UDS default. Node dials TCP in both lanes.
notes:
Adapter side done (TCP listen; stdio bridge removed). REMAINING (node side): runc lane dial TCP + delete Docker-API stdio attach.
STARTED + reverted to keep tree green. DIRECTION CONFIRMED: make DockerPodBackend mirror CRIPodBackend (TCP). Exact prod change (small): docker_pod.go StartAgent -> add Env ACP_LISTEN=tcp://0.0.0.0:7000 + AttachStdio:false; Attach -> AttachTCP(podIP:7000); add shared runtime.ACPPort=7000 (cri's acpPort already 7000; AttachACP/abstract-UDS appears DEAD). BLOCKERS needing the real docker/runsc harness + test redesign (why reverted): (1) two integration tests model stdio via FakeRuntime.Attach echo — TestDockerPodBackendAttachUsesRuntimeAttach + TestWSRelayEchoesViaFake — must be reworked to a loopback-TCP 'agent'; (2) fixed port 7000 makes loopback tests collide across packages -> make the port injectable (DockerPodBackend field; FakeRuntime.ContainerIP->127.0.0.1); (3) rootless-podman-without-bridge-IP can't be reached via podIP:port -> needs published-port plumbing or declare unsupported; validate on real bridge docker + runsc; (4) episode-end FSM (sp-5h3.5 leftover): long-lived adapter means node<->adapter EOF is reconnectable, not spawn-over.
close_reason: Both lanes unified on TCP ACP. DockerPodBackend sets ACP_LISTEN=tcp + dials podIP:ACPPort via AttachTCP (mirrors CRI); stdio hijack removed; shared runtime.ACPPort; injectable port for tests. Tests reworked to TCP (loopback agent + transport-agnostic relay). Full suite green. VALIDATED end-to-end with the real image on rootful docker: host dialed pod bridge IP 172.17.0.6:7000 -> adapter -> opencode -> sidecar -> OpenRouter -> 'PONG'. (Episode-end FSM nuance tracked under sp-5h3.5; rootless-without-bridge-IP noted unsupported.)

title: Adapter: discover-or-create single opencode session per spawn (resume-safe)
description: On container start the adapter GET /session: reuse the persisted session if present (after resume), else create one. Node's session/new maps onto it; TUI attaches and lands on the only session (no reliance on 'opencode attach --session', unreliable per #5445/#7149). Naive always-create would orphan restored history on resume.
close_reason: Implemented + tested: DiscoverOrCreateSession (dir-scoped, resume-safe); adapter maps session/new onto it

title: opencode adapter: ACP-agent server backed by opencode (normalize to canonical ACP)
description: Rewrite deploy/agent/acpadapter into an ACP-agent server speaking CANONICAL ACP to the node, backed by opencode HTTP/SSE. Map: initialize<->health+discover-or-create session; session/new<->the single session; session/prompt + streamed session/update (agent_message_chunk/agent_thought_chunk/tool)<->prompt_async + /event SSE deltas; session/request_permission (options w/ ACP kinds allow_*/reject_*)<->permission.asked + POST permissions; busy/idle<->session.status/session.idle; cancel<->abort. Subscribe /event once, fan ALL events (incl TUI-originated) to ACP session/update. Reconnect+backfill via GET messages (Go SDK has no auto-reconnect). Synthesize ACP turn state for TUI-originated turns so node busy/queue stays correct; map opencode busy errors back to ACP. Keep at-most-one node conn + gap buffer.
notes: CORE DONE + verified vs real opencode 1.15.13: ACP server (initialize/session-new/prompt+streaming via message.part.delta routed by partID->type, session.idle->turn-end, permission.asked->session/request_permission with ACP kinds->POST permissions, cancel->abort, SSE reconnect). Binary rewired off stdio bridge. REMAINING: busy synthesis for TUI turns; ToolPart->tool_call mapping.

title: Agent image: ship opencode + tmux; pin version; sidecar provider config; PID1 supervision
description: Replace goose with a pinned opencode in the agent image; also install tmux (for Phase 2). Entrypoint runs 'opencode serve --port 4096 --hostname 127.0.0.1' AND the rewritten adapter under a minimal supervisor (tini/sh) as PID1. Point opencode's provider at the existing sidecar OpenAI-compat endpoint (127.0.0.1:8080) + injected key (replace goose env wiring in entrypoint.sh; sidecar unchanged). If opencode crashes, the adapter must fail its ACP conn so the node sees agent-down (no silent zombie).
close_reason: Agent image built (opencode 1.15.13 + tmux + tini), entrypoint runs opencode serve + adapter with a generated sidecar-backed opencode provider config. VALIDATED end-to-end in a podman pod: real ACP prompt streamed 'PONG' via adapter->opencode->sidecar->OpenRouter.

title: EPIC: opencode swap + ACP as canonical agent protocol (Phase 1)
description: Replace goose with opencode (headless 'opencode serve' HTTP/SSE) behind a per-agent in-pod adapter that normalizes opencode's API to CANONICAL ACP. web/CP/node speak only neutral spec ACP; vendor churn is quarantined in adapters. This is the foundation for onboarding many agents (opencode now, goose as near pass-through/neutrality witness, future agents later). Phase 1 makes the web path work end-to-end with opencode; no CP/web changes, only small agent-neutral node cleanups. See spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-opencode-swap-and-terminal-design.md.

title: [demo-mvp] Health + readiness endpoints (CP /healthz, /readyz; node liveness)
description: No health endpoints today. Add CP /healthz (process up) + /readyz (store reachable, registry initialized). Surface node liveness/last-seen (the attach-stream heartbeat already exists — expose it) so the CP/operator sees which nodes are alive. Lets the demo confirm the stack before spawning and shows a node drop.
acceptance_criteria: CP /healthz=200 when up; /readyz reflects store+registry; node last-seen queryable/logged; a killed node shows stale last-seen within the heartbeat window.

title: [demo-mvp] Core metrics + /metrics endpoint (CP + node)
description: No metrics today (no prometheus/expvar/otel). Add a lightweight metrics surface + /metrics on CP and node. Series: spawns_created/active/failed, spawn_start_duration histogram, nodes_connected gauge + connect/disconnect + per-node active/capacity, sessions_active + frames_relayed (pump throughput), egress_floor_apply/remove/fail, cp_rpc_requests/latency/errors by method. Directly surfaces the sp-9um (relay bandwidth) and sp-8hf (container-leak) risk classes.
acceptance_criteria: GET /metrics on CP+node returns the listed series; active-spawns gauge returns to zero after teardown (catches leaks); spawn_start_duration visible; floor_fail increments on a fail-closed event.

title: [demo-mvp] Telemetry event coverage — failures + full lifecycle (content-free)
description: internal/cp/telemetry emits only spawn_create, session_start, session_end. Add events to show the real lifecycle AND failures on stage: spawn_active, spawn_error (coded reason, no content), spawn_stop/suspend/resume, node_connect, node_disconnect, floor_apply, floor_remove. Emit from the CP server lifecycle + node attach paths. Content-free (ids/timestamps/reasons only).
acceptance_criteria: A failing spawn emits spawn_create->spawn_error(reason); a node drop emits node_disconnect; floor apply/remove recorded per pod; events stay content-free; telemetry_test covers the new kinds.

title: [demo-mvp] Structured logging (slog) with correlation ids across CP + node
description: Replace the ~48 ad-hoc stdlib log.* call sites (cmd/, internal/) with structured slog: consistent levels and correlation fields on every spawn/session-scoped line — spawn_id, node_id, owner, client_id. Every lifecycle transition and error path should log once with context (keep the node's logErr+debug.Stack but structure it). Content-free (no prompt/reply text).
acceptance_criteria: All log sites go through a shared slog logger; spawn/session logs carry spawn_id+node_id so grepping one spawn id traces its whole lifecycle across CP+node; levels consistent; no prompt/reply content in logs.

title: [demo-mvp] Observability coverage for the MVP demo — metrics, structured logs, telemetry
description: As-built observability is too thin for a live demo. Telemetry (internal/cp/telemetry) emits only spawn_create / session_start / session_end (content-free JSONL, CP-side). Logging is 48 ad-hoc stdlib log.* call sites (unstructured, no correlation ids, inconsistent levels). There are ZERO metrics (no prometheus/expvar/otel, no /metrics) and no health/readiness endpoints. For the demo we need enough signal to (a) show the happy-path spawn lifecycle, (b) SEE failures on stage — node disconnect, spawn error, egress-floor apply/break, container/relay leaks (the sp-8hf / sp-9um classes), and (c) let an operator confirm the stack is up. Children split this into: core metrics + /metrics, structured logging, telemetry event coverage, health/readiness, and a demo-time live view.
design: Keep all observability CONTENT-FREE (mirror the telemetry no-prompt/no-reply rule). Correlate on ids only: spawn_id, node_id, owner, client_id. Cover both CP and node. Scope to what makes the demo legible + debuggable on stage, not full prod SLOs.

title: Long-lived per-spawn pump + multi-client session (fix relay-lifecycle race)
description: Replace the per-WS-connection goose attach + brokerEndpoint relay with a long-lived per-spawn pump (node) that owns the single goose ACP session, fans out an append-only frame log to N clients with resumable per-client cursors, and survives client reconnects/zero-clients/CP-restart. Fixes the reconnect-churn race (leaked relays, dropped inbox, stuck 'working...') diagnosed 2026-06-03. Subsumes sp-r7t (node owns one session) and replaces sp-39u's standalone readiness probe. Spans proto (client_id+cursor), CP router (multi-client), CP ws.go, node pump, web thin client. Docker lane. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-spawn-pump-multiclient-design.md. Plans: pump core, then integration.
close_reason: Long-lived per-spawn pump + multi-client session SHIPPED (Plans 1+2). Pump (internal/node) owns one goose ACP session, append-only frame log fanned out to N clients with resumable per-client cursors, broker turn/queue, transient permission broadcast (first-wins/timeout-deny). Integration: client_id+cursor proto; multi-client CP router; node attach rewired onto the pump (pump.start=readiness gate, replacing sp-39u probe; agent-death reclaims); web thin frame client (prompt/subscribe, per-tab clientId+cursor, no ACP). Fixes the reconnect-churn race (leaked relays/dropped inbox/stuck 'working...') structurally. 4+6 tasks two-stage reviewed; final reviews READY-TO-MERGE; full go suite + 77 web vitest + e2e 13/13 green. Subsumed sp-r7t (node owns one session) + sp-39u probe. Merged to master.

title: Spawn lifecycle slice 3: web multi-spawn UI (list, status dots, kebab actions, switch + replay)
description: App.tsx multi-spawn state (spawns Map + activeId, one live ws for activeId); ListSpawns hydrate + ~3s poll. Sidebar 'Spawns' list: status dot (green active/grey suspended/red errored-unreachable/amber transitional), name headline + app-id subline, kebab Rename/Suspend|Resume/Stop, double-click inline rename. Switch closes ws, restores items, opens ws if active, onHistory replaces items. api: listSpawns/renameSpawn/suspendSpawn/resumeSpawn/deleteSpawn. vitest + Playwright e2e (two secret-app instances, rename, suspend/resume, stop, switch+replay). See spec §Slice 3 / epic sp-k20.
close_reason: Slice 3 done: multi-spawn sidebar + App state (buffer switch-history, 3s poll, onHistory) + api + e2e (9/9 real containers). Merged to master (abff754).

title: Spawn lifecycle slice 2: acpadapter history replay (recording proxy + spawn/history + client onHistory)
description: acpadapter becomes an ACP-aware line-oriented recording proxy (ndjson): parse both directions, forward verbatim, record a coalesced transcript (user/agent/thought/tool, 500-item cap + truncation marker). On client attach, emit one out-of-band {method:spawn/history,params:{items}} frame. web/src/acp/client.ts gains onHistory + a route branch. Visual transcript only; lost on suspend. See spec §Slice 2 / epic sp-k20.
close_reason: Slice 2 done (re-close after .beads merge-import reverted it): acpadapter recording proxy + spawn/history replay + client onHistory/historyToItems. Merged to master.

title: Spawn lifecycle slice 1: backend (name persistence + RenameSpawn + Suspend/Resume + Stop)
description: Pure CP/store, hermetic. Proto additive: SpawnSummary.name, CreateSpawnRequest.name, RenameSpawn RPC; implement SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn (today Unimplemented). Store migration 0004: spawns.name + UNIQUE(owner_id,name) over non-deleted; Rename + suspend/resume guarded transitions (reuse Suspending/Suspended enum + existing teardown/provision). CreateSpawn server-side name autogen from app display name + collision counter suffix. UI Stop -> DeleteSpawn (soft-delete). See spec §Slice 1 / epic sp-k20.
close_reason: Slice 1 backend complete: proto name+RenameSpawn, migration 0004, store.Rename, name autogen, Rename/Suspend/Resume handlers. 8 tasks, two-stage reviewed, final review READY TO MERGE. Merged to master (5fba342).

title: Spawn lifecycle UI: multi-instance, named, suspend/stop, status, history replay
description: Make spawns first-class in the web UI: multiple instances of the same app, backend-persisted independent names (autogen + rename), suspend/resume + stop controls, sidebar status dots, and transcript replay on switch. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-spawn-lifecycle-ui-design.md. 3 slices (backend lifecycle / adapter history replay / web multi-spawn UI).
close_reason: All 3 demo-scope build slices done + merged (sp-zer backend, sp-02f adapter replay, sp-r6b web multi-spawn UI). Tests green (go + web unit/tsc + 9/9 e2e). Post-demo work tracked separately (sp-3nb durable history, sp-suc pagination, sp-1ni suspend reconcile).

title: Per-backend ACP transport for rootless self-hosted (Docker attach + UDS) + empty-IP tolerance
description: GOAL: rootless self-hosted nodes on Mac AND Linux (incl. rootless Podman via Docker-compat socket); root only for cloud nodes is fine. PROBLEM: slice 1 (sp-7wa) unified BOTH backends on the UDS @spawnlet-acp + setns transport, which needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN (root) AND cannot cross the Docker Desktop macOS<->Linux-VM boundary (no setns on macOS; Unix sockets don't traverse virtiofs). So self-hosted-without-root and Mac Docker Desktop are both BROKEN today. FIX (per-backend transport): (1) add PodBackend.Attach(ctx,*PodHandle)(*AttachedStream,error); Docker impl = restore Docker stdio attach (rt.Attach(agentID), Mac+Linux, root-free, over the Docker API); CRI impl = AttachACP(netnsPath) (Linux+cloud+root, unchanged). (2) agent image entrypoint conditional on ACP_ADAPTER env: unset -> exec goose acp (PID1=goose, Docker-attachable); CRI backend sets ACP_ADAPTER=1 -> runs behind the adapter on the in-pod UDS. stubagent entrypoint -> direct (Docker-attachable). (3) route relay via Manager.Attach(ctx,sp)->m.pod.Attach; update server.go/ws.go/node/attach.go. (4) EMPTY-IP TOLERANCE (subsumes sp-mcb): DockerPodBackend.StartPod tolerates empty ContainerIP (rootless Podman slirp4netns/pasta has no bridge IP); Manager.Create fails-closed only if egressEnforced() && PodIP=='' . (5) docs: rootless self-hosted matrix (Docker / rootless Docker / rootless Podman DOCKER_HOST=podman.sock), update slice-1 CAP_SYS_ADMIN claims (now CRI-lane-only). RESULT: self-hosted Mac/Linux/Podman = no root; cloud CRI = root for floor only.
design: Per-backend transport. Reverses slice-1 unify-on-UDS (which broke non-root + Mac). Docker lane = Docker attach (no setns, no port, Mac via Docker API); CRI lane = UDS+setns (cloud, Linux, root). ACP_ADAPTER env toggles the agent entrypoint. Empty-IP tolerance needed for rootless Podman.
close_reason: DONE merged 80d1767. Per-backend ACP transport: Docker/runc lane = Docker stdio attach (rt.Attach, root-free, Mac+Linux+rootless Podman); CRI/runsc lane = UDS+setns (Linux+cloud). PodBackend.Attach + Manager.Attach routes the relay (Server.attach injection removed). Agent entrypoint toggles ACP_ADAPTER (Docker=goose PID1 attachable; CRI sets ACP_ADAPTER=1 -> adapter); stubagent direct. Empty pod IP tolerated (rootless Podman slirp4netns), Manager fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP=='' (closes sp-mcb; CRI still requires IP). Image Go builders bumped to golang:1.26. title: [bug] egress floor blocks DNS when resolvers are RFC1918 (breaks inference)
description: HOST-VERIFIED finding (sp-rpa): on a host whose DNS resolvers are RFC1918 (e.g. 192.168.1.x home LAN, or cloud internal DNS), the egress block-floor's blanket RFC1918 DROP kills DNS resolution. Verified on the dev host: metadata+RFC1918 correctly blocked, public-by-IP (1.1.1.1) reachable, but DNS (resolvers 192.168.1.192/1.1) blocked -> curl 'could not resolve host'. DEMO-BREAKING: the sidecar resolves api.openrouter.ai, so inference breaks on every cloud spawn on such a host. FIX: firewall.Rules allow udp/tcp dport 53 ACCEPT before the RFC1918/metadata drops (standard egress-floor DNS carve-out). Residual: DNS-tunneling/internal-DNS-recon possible (acceptable for demo; audit layer covers content). Found by host-verification of the egress floor.
close_reason: Done+host-verified, merged 72256cf: added udp/tcp :53 ACCEPT before the RFC1918/metadata drops in firewall.Rules (resolvers are commonly RFC1918 -> blanket drop broke DNS -> broke sidecar->OpenRouter inference). egress_e2e now checks public egress by IP (1.1.1.1, DNS-independent) and PASSES on a privileged host; metadata+RFC1918 counter-proven dropped, DNS :53 counter-proven ACCEPTed. just test-egress recipe added. Unit test asserts DNS-before-drops.

Run the build-tagged test on a privileged host with Docker+iptables+root: 'go test -tags egress_e2e ./internal/spawnlet/firewall/ -run TestEgressFloorEnforced'. Asserts 169.254.169.254 + RFC1918 blocked, public (openrouter) reachable. Also confirm a real spawn pod (sidecar+agent shared netns) is firewalled end to end. title: Spawn lifecycle: active<->suspended, resume, idle-suspend, delete
description: Make the spawn lifecycle explicit and implement it: spawns are durable, resumable instances (not one-shot sessions). States starting/active/suspending/suspended/error/deleted. Operations: create, attach (auto-resume on open, single-session takeover), detach (container keeps running), suspend (explicit stop OR two-stage per-node idle timeout), resume (re-provision container w/ same config+backends), delete (preserve data backend by default). Suspend/resume is DATA-ONLY: full working tree incl uncommitted work persisted via a git WIP ref (refs/spawnery/suspend/<id>) that rides the existing GitHub-push / git-bundle-all paths; restored as unstaged on resume; fresh ACP session. CP/node restart reconciles in-flight/orphaned spawns to suspended. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-spawn-lifecycle-design.md (authoritative for the state machine; amends system-design 2/3). Touches CP index/DAO (status enum + fields), E0 contracts (ListSpawns/SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/DeleteSpawn; Session auto-resumes; StopSpawn->suspend), node agent (clean-suspend/resume/idle timer), web client (list+status+controls).
design: Data-only resume (conversation continuity deferred). WIP-ref chosen over tar-archive (git-native, reuses both persist paths, bundle --all captures it free) and over stash-precise (less plumbing; dirty restored unstaged, fine for an agent). Idle = two-stage per-node: detached-short, attached-idle-long; relay frame in either direction = activity (node doesn't parse ACP). Single active container per spawn; single session w/ takeover. Delete preserves data by default ('your data is yours'); destroy is opt-in. HARD DEP: persistent storage backend (E3 managed) — resume is meaningless on Scratch. Honesty caveat: clean suspend lossless; hard crash lossy-to-last-checkpoint.
notes: HANDOFF from sp-8hf (2026-06-05): two pieces of sp-gd9 scope now have foundations in place. (a) Generation write-fence: node-side staleGen helper (internal/node/attach.go) + Manager.SpawnGeneration already fence stale-gen Stop messages; when the Suspend CPMessage handler lands here it should call a.staleGen too (currently Suspend is an inert TODO in handle's default case). (b) Idle timer: a LOSSY two-stage idle reaper shipped in sp-8hf (Pump.lastActivity + attached(), attacher.reapIdle/idleReapLoop, 15m detached/60m attached, reports STOPPED). This issue's job is to upgrade it to LOSSLESS suspend-on-idle (persist via git WIP-ref, report SUSPENDED) once E3 persistent storage lands.

title: Review authn/authz model — ensure every API is properly authorized
description:
Cross-cutting security review of the demo MVP's auth model: every API surface must authenticate the caller AND authorize the specific action/resource (no IDOR, no unauthenticated mutations). Audit each seam:

CLIENT-FACING (CP HTTP/OpenAPI):
- CP index API (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /spawns, POST /spawns/{id}/session): OWNERSHIP-scope every call — a user can only list/open/stop/delete THEIR OWN spawns. Guessing a spawnId must not grant access (IDOR).
- Catalog/registry API (GET /apps, POST /apps publish, ratings/flags/takedown): visibility scoping (private apps need entitlement); only the creator/owner can publish/modify their app; creator-capability gate.
- Session-token issuance: only the spawn owner gets a token for that spawn; token scoped to {spawnId, owner, node, exp}.

SERVICE-TO-SERVICE:
- CP<->node gRPC: mTLS + node identity bound to owner (per E4 §6); a node can't act for another's spawns; service-token validity/rotation.
- spawnlet ConnectRPC (CreateSpawn/Session/StopSpawn): TODAY THE SLICE HAS ZERO AUTH — anyone who can reach the spawnlet can create/attach/stop ANY spawn id. In production these come from the CP, so the spawnlet must authorize that requests originate from the legit CP (and Session must verify the session token binds the caller to the spawn). CLOSE THIS GAP when CP integration lands.
- Storage-token minting: CP-minted tokens scoped to exactly the user's repo (no over-broad scope); per E3 §3.

DATA/CONTROL:
- Per-app permissions + spawn-time consent + egress enforcement (sp-ba5) — the consent record must actually gate what the spawn can do.
- Audit-store break-glass access gated + logged (E8 §1); product-metrics (sp-yjw) content-free.
- Trust-tier capabilities enforced (unverified/scanned/reviewed gates what an app may request).

DELIVERABLE: a checklist of every endpoint x {authn? authz/ownership? IDOR-safe?}, gaps filed as issues, and the spawnlet-has-no-auth gap explicitly tracked to close at CP-integration time. Related: E0 contracts (sp-9zo), E4 identity (sp-7h6), E8 (sp-ydz); roast sp-gtm/sp-ba5/sp-gl2.

title: Concierge pre-build retention test (10-20 users, hand-held)
description: The cheapest test of the riskiest assumption (does a representative user retain to a personal-agent app), runnable NOW before the platform build. Recruit 10-20 Cohort-A/B target users (NOT viral-tweet crowd); for 2-3 weeks hand-run the top 1-2 candidate apps (Wiki+Research + Language Partner) with a HIGH-QUALITY model + manual glue (shared chat + manually maintained per-user state). Measure: unprompted week-2 return + qual (felt like mine? keep using? pay?). Decision rule: if they won't return when hand-held + excellent, the polished B+Y version won't save it -> don't build that app as flagship; pivot. ~0 engineering; removes all confounders (model/latency/onboarding). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-spawnery-demo-experiment-design.md §4.

title: Event cold-storage telemetry (hourly Parquet, content-free)
description: The CP emits SESSION-LIFECYCLE events to cold storage as HOURLY PARQUET files for offline metric analysis: who created which app session, when (spawn create/start/end, owner, app@sha, trust tier, storage type, node placement) -- METADATA ONLY, NO conversation/data content. PRINCIPLE (firm): product/engagement metrics are derived ONLY from these content-free events; we do NOT inspect user /data (e.g. wiki page counts) for engagement. This keeps the metrics pipeline cleanly SEPARATE from the abuse-audit pipeline (E8: content, 30d, break-glass, different purpose + access path). Enables offline retention / activation / app-popularity / 3rd-party-spawn-share analysis without touching user data. Emitter = CP (it owns session lifecycle). Affects E8 (separation note), CP/E1.
notes: Experiment design needs turn_count + session_end added to the event schema (session-level metadata counters, NOT content) for activation + retention metrics. See demo-experiment-design.md §2/§7.

title: DEMO-ROAST: GitHub-only storage inverts the target market
description: Demo storage = GitHub, but the pitch is NON-TECHNICAL users who don't have GitHub accounts and won't authorize an App grant. So the only people who can onboard are developers -- the segment LEAST impressed by a hosted wiki (they self-host). The demo is hardest for the intended user and least compelling for the reachable one. FIX: ship MANAGED storage (Spawnery-hosted, open-format, exportable) as the DEFAULT, with 'connect your own GitHub' as the power-user option (architecture already supports managed storage). Default any spawned/managed repo to PRIVATE (no public-repo abuse as free CDN). Affects E3, E6 onboarding, demo-mvp-scope, system §5.
notes: DECIDED: managed storage = DEFAULT (web-browsable + download/clone); Drive/iCloud/OneDrive (git bundle) = BYO-cloud option; GitHub = power-user. Spawned repos default private. Scope doc §4 updated.

title: DEMO-ROAST: demo is a weak experiment — doesn't test the thesis; no success metric
description: The business bet is differentiation (your-data + your-model + creator marketplace). The demo has no model choice (DeepSeek only), undercut data ownership (CP holds GitHub token, all audited), and ZERO marketplace (4 first-party apps, no creators). So a demo win only proves 'free hosted AI wiki is mildly appealing' (already suggested by NotebookLM/Mem/GPT-Store) and a loss is ambiguous (idea vs gutted execution). Also no defined success metric/audience/threshold => the experiment can't fail, so it can't inform go/no-go. And no retention hypothesis (why open the wiki on day 8?). FIX: define the explicit demo hypothesis + metric + audience before build; add a retention hook (e.g. weekly 'capture what you learned' nudge); be honest this validates one app's stickiness, not the platform.
notes:
ELABORATION (3 layers):
1) THESIS COVERAGE: tests ~1 of 5 load-bearing bets. H1 (wiki demand) weakly/confounded; H2 (data+model differentiation) NO; H3 (creators publish) NO; H4 (marketplace flywheel) NO; H5 (non-technical onboarding) anti-tested (GitHub gate filters them out).
2) CONFOUNDING: demo changes 5 vars at once vs ideal product (weak model, cold-start latency, GitHub wall, capped access, unrepresentative audience) => low retention is unattributable (idea? model? latency? onboarding? audience?). Unreadable signal.
3) NO METRIC/THRESHOLD/KILL-CRITERION: viral-tweet acquisition => will measure signups (vanity; measures tweet reach not product value). No pre-committed 'if X<Y by Z we do NOT build' => un-failable => can't inform go/no-go.
KEY RISK: the WIKI may retain WORST (PKM cold-start-value death spiral: only valuable once big, won't invest before valuable). The thing that breaks the spiral = proactive agent synthesis = the real differentiator = NOT in demo scope. So demo may ship the non-magical wiki and wrongly conclude 'wikis don't retain'. Irony: the coaches (cut by sp-jg3) have STRONGER built-in return loops (habit=daily, interview=event-driven) than the flagship.
META: demo conflates (a) technical proof [vertical slice does this, low risk] with (b) market validation [needs experiment design]. Don't read (a)'s usage as (b).
CONSTRUCTIVE: (i) name the single riskiest assumption (likely: does a personal LLM wiki retain a representative user past W2); (ii) test it CONCIERGE-style with 10-20 target non-technical users + high-quality model + manual glue BEFORE/around the build (~0 eng, removes confounders); (iii) pre-commit metric (activation + W2/W4 retention, NOT signups) + threshold + audience + decision; (iv) if building B+Y anyway, kill cheap confounders (managed storage sp-0a2, model-quality floor, proactive-synthesis if retention is what you measure).
UPDATE: open marketplace now IN demo scope -> the demo tests a slice of H3/H4 (do creators publish? do users spawn 3rd-party apps?). Improves thesis coverage from ~1/5 to ~2.5/5. STILL UNSOLVED: no success metric/threshold/representative-audience, and the wiki PKM cold-start-value retention risk. Define the experiment before build.
REFRAMED (user): the LLM Wiki is NOT the main bet -- the ARCHITECTURE across a portfolio of personal-agent ideas is. title: DEMO-ROAST: pod resource limits + per-user quotas + isolation hardening
description: Single GPU box + plain containers + free tier = compute-abuse + free-inference-farming + DoS. One abuser can exhaust the GPU and deny the whole free tier (capacity ceiling = attack surface). FIX: cgroup CPU/mem/disk/pids limits on every pod (stop mining/fork-bombs/disk-fill); per-user concurrent-spawn cap; per-user daily token/inference quota; reconsider gVisor for the demo (cheap-ish, given the agent runs untrusted-user-steered code). Sybil friction: prefer GitHub-auth (more friction/age signal than throwaway Gmail) or a phone/age gate before free inference. Affects E1, E8 §3 free-tier signals.
close_reason: Done, merged 597e0fb: per-spawn cgroup limits (mem/cpu/pids via buildHostConfig on both pod containers, defaults 1024MB/1CPU/256, env MEM_LIMIT_MB/CPU_LIMIT/PIDS_LIMIT) + optional gVisor runtime (CONTAINER_RUNTIME, opt-in) + per-user concurrent-spawn cap (CreateSpawn checkSpawnQuota -> ResourceExhausted, CP_MAX_SPAWNS_PER_OWNER default 5, SetMaxSpawnsPerOwner setter). Hermetic tests (buildHostConfig mapping, manager applies to both specs+defaults, cap via helper+CreateSpawn) green; -race clean. deployment.md updated. CAVEAT: real cgroup/gVisor enforcement unverified in sandbox (verify on node host). NOTE: sp-eha's restricted-agent-toolset piece still pending (separate).

title: ROAST: GitHub polling + per-turn persist = rate limits & I/O cliff
description: Found by ops #6/#7. (a) CP polls GitHub for latest tag->SHA at EVERY createSpawn + auto-resume: adds a sync round-trip, ~5000/hr installation rate-limit shared with provisioning+clone+push, and a hard dependency (GitHub down => no new auto spawns). (b) Persist per turn = git push every turn (rate-limit pressure) and for blob, 'git bundle --all' + FULL re-upload of entire history every turn => latency/bandwidth grow linearly with repo size (100MB+ per turn for an old spawn). FIX: cache resolved tag->SHA w/ TTL (periodic poll populates it; createSpawn never blocks on GitHub; fallback to last-known-good when GitHub down); debounce/coalesce persistence + background push; incremental blob bundles (since last ref) or pull folder-as-git-remote into MVP for blob. Affects E5 §2, E3 §2/§5.
close_reason: Demo-relevant doc-fix pass applied (commit pending): honest framing/open-core/portability/consent-deferred/storage-token honesty in system-design; stale E2E/bridge-cert refs reconciled (system §4/§10/log, E0 §7, E2.2); contract drift fixed (E0 §5a/§6 createSpawn-vs-startSpawn, refreshStorageToken, dropped sessionTokenPubkey, async POST /spawns); GitHub poll-cache + persist debounce + incremental blob (E5/E3); ad-hoc URL SSRF guard (E5); classifier-off-GPU + audit-store sizing (E8); DR seams noted (system §4).

Also no epic OWNS egress enforcement when it lands, and BYO-direct inference means the allowlist is per-spawn-dynamic (depends on the BYO baseUrl), not static 'provider+storage only'. FIX: align system §9 to post-MVP; assign egress-enforcement ownership to E1 (pod netns layer), configured from the manifest permission block + the spawn's model baseUrl; note the dynamic-allowlist complication. Affects system §9, E0 §9, E1.
notes: REOPENED + UN-DEFERRED: open third-party marketplace requires the manifest permissions block + spawn-time consent + egress ENFORCEMENT in the demo. Was incorrectly deferred when scope was first-party-only.

title: ROAST: open-core 'single-box docker-compose' claim is false; portability oversold
description: Found by arch #6, product F12. system §1 still says self-host 'runs identically as a single-box docker compose stack' but E0.10 made the CP a single central managed instance: a self-hosted node is INERT without the CP (can't enroll, get session tokens, resolve apps, route managed inference; the only client is the CP-served SPA). Also 'carry your repo to ANY Spawnery and re-spawn identically' — there is only ONE CP (self-hosted CP post-MVP), and spawn.yml references a CREATOR's definition repo you don't control (delist/delete => can't reconstruct). FIX: rewrite system §1 open-core constraint (self-host = node attaching to central CP; standalone post-MVP); split portability claim into 'content fully portable (plain files)' vs 'spawn config runs on Spawnery'; let users snapshot the App definition into their own repo so a delisted app still re-spawns. Affects system §1/§2/§11/§12.

title: ROAST: storage-token custody undercuts 'your data is yours'
description: Found by arch #9, product F1. CP holds the GitHub App signer + OAuth refresh tokens and can mint read/write tokens to the user's repo at will, silently, server-side, no per-action consent, no user-visible log. The E2E machinery protects the model KEY but not the DATA (the bigger asset). FIX: reconcile the top-level claim (narrow to 'CP never sees conversation content; storage access is CP-mediated via scoped tokens in MVP'); ship a user-visible storage-access log (every token mint + push, with reason) in MVP; surface CP storage access in E8 transparency UI; pull user-held/E2E-sealed storage creds forward if feasible. Affects E3 §3, E8 §6, system §1/§5.

title: ROAST: 'one-click' is a lie — onboarding gauntlet for non-technical user
description: Found by product F7/F8/F13. First-run for the target user: OAuth + invent a vault passphrase + store a 24-word BIP-39 mnemonic + approve a GitHub per-repo grant + a 4-field wizard (App version / choose agent / choose model / choose storage) + cold-start wait. ~6-7 decision points, two of them (passphrase, seed phrase) alien concepts, for a user who wanted 'make a wiki'. 'Choose agent' is meaningless to them. FIX: genuine one-click default (managed storage + managed model + recommended agent); LAZY vault (no passphrase until first BYO key — free tier never needs it); hide agent choice behind Advanced; if a passphrase is set, make recovery codes mandatory not opt-in. Affects E6 §3, E4 §1/§3/§5, system §1.
notes: Largely RESOLVED by demo cut: no vault/BYOK -> onboarding = OAuth + connect GitHub + spawn.

title: ROAST: coding-agent-as-coach is unvalidated and load-bearing
description: Found by product F10 (arch #5). E7 §6 itself flags that a coding-style agent is tuned to 'do a file task,' not conversational coaching — then punts. 3 of 4 launch apps (habit/goal worst, sysdesign best, wiki fine) coerce a task-executor into a conversationalist via persona alone. A habit coach that answers 'I'm struggling' by grepping checkins/ and committing a file is uncanny — and it's the app that defines the emotional brand. FIX: dogfood the habit coach with real non-technical users for ~2 weeks; measure 'felt like a coach vs a robot'; be ready to drop habit coach from launch. Don't ship the weakest-fit app to prove generality. Affects E7 §1/§4/§6.
notes:
Doc gate strengthened in E7 §6 (dogfood habit coach ~2wk + free-DeepSeek compat check). Issue stays OPEN as real pre-launch validation work, not closable by docs.
E11 RESOLUTION: habit/goal coach DROPPED from the seed lineup (worst uncanny fit; needed proactive nudges, deferred). Seed coaches = Language Partner (tutor/DRILL role -> task-shaped, dodges the uncanny-warm-coach risk) + Interview Coach (structured task). The sp-pgw risk is largely sidestepped by choosing task-shaped roles over warm-coaching. Remaining validation: language tutoring quality on DeepSeek-Flash (sp-iui).

title: ROAST: vault hardening — recovery codes, KDF, Argon2 params
description: Found by security #4/#5 (product F13). (a) Multi-use recovery codes stored as wrap_rc at the CP = standing, non-expiring, undetectable skeleton key; CP compromise + the code = all BYO secrets forever, silently. (b) KDF(RC) is unspecified (only the passphrase path specifies Argon2id). (c) Argon2id 64MiB/t=3 tuned for browser perf is weak for operator-held ciphertext; no passphrase strength policy anywhere. (d) Recovery codes opt-in => non-technical user silently bricks vault. FIX: single-use recovery codes (invalidate wrap_rc on use); specify KDF(RC) (Argon2id or HKDF w/ domain separation); enforce passphrase strength floor + raise memory cost w/ progress UI; notify on any wrap_rc fetch; consider offline-only recovery (never upload wrap_rc). Affects E4 §3/§3a/§4/§8.

title: (EPIC) [demo-mvp] Spawn-view tab bar: multiple sessions per spawn (agents + shell)
description:
Add a tab bar atop the web UI's spawn view so one spawn can host MULTIPLE concurrent sessions inside its ONE container (shared filesystem + sidecar). Tab 1 = the current primary surface (native web ChatView for acp/served, term.js TUI for tmux). A '+' button opens a new tab that is either: (a) a NEW AGENT SESSION of a user-chosen runnable (acp-mode -> its own native-web ChatView tab; tui-mode -> its own term.js/tmux tab), or (b) a term.js BASH SHELL into the spawn's container.

DECISIONS (owner, 2026-06-07): tabs map to multiple sessions WITHIN the single spawn container (NOT separate spawns) — additional sessions launched via docker exec into the running container, reusing the terminal-attach PTY path. MVP scope INCLUDES native-web ACP additional agents (each needs its own in-container ACP endpoint + a node Pump + a ChatView tab), plus term.js shell and tui-agent tabs.

This is a real backend feature, not just UI: today a spawn runs ONE primary agent (entrypoint dispatches one runnable). Multi-session-per-spawn needs: a per-spawn session registry + lifecycle (create/list/close), the node launching additional sessions into a running container (shell via docker exec; tui-agent via docker exec spawn-tmux <runnable> in a uniquely-named tmux session; acp-agent via a second acpmux/acp endpoint on a fresh port with its own node Pump), proto/RPC session CRUD threaded CP<->node, the web tab bar + per-tab surface wiring (multiple concurrent ChatView/TerminalView sockets bound to session ids), and reuse of the per-runnable launch/env setup (opencode sidecar config, goose env, claude apiKeyHelper, tmux wrapping — factor out of the entrypoint dispatcher so exec-launched sessions get the same tweaks). Coordinate with the URL-history work (sp-jpn) for per-tab deep links and the Marketplace->Templates rename (sp-u5q). Rough child tasks filed; impl plans per task.
