title: claude-acp: wrap the Claude Agent SDK in an ACP-stdio shim (native web + shared nori terminal)
description:
Add a 'claude-acp' runnable: a small ACP-over-stdio shim backed by the Anthropic Claude Agent SDK (programmatic agent SDK, formerly Claude Code SDK; TS @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk or Python). The shim speaks canonical ACP (initialize / session/new / session/prompt / session/update incl. tool_call CONTENT + session/request_permission, and ideally the richer variants — plans, diffs, etc.) and maps it to the SDK's streaming query + tool/permission callbacks. Analogous to ocadapter (opencode HTTP+SSE -> ACP) but for the Claude Agent SDK.

PAYOFF: gives Claude the SAME dual-surface goose-acp has, which today it lacks (Claude is tmux-only via claude-tui, no rich web): (1) NATIVE WEB ChatView — claude-acp -> node pump / acpmux -> web; (2) nori in the TERMINAL backed by Claude, sharing the SAME live conversation via acpdial -> acpmux. Reuses the sp-9xr.12 machinery (acpmux single-session multiplexer + nori acpdial shared attach) verbatim — claude-acp is just another ModeACP/RelayPump runnable.

COMPOSITION/DEPS: register a runnable in internal/agentcaps (e.g. claude-code -> claude-acp, ModeACP, RelayPump) + a dispatcher case (run the shim, multiplexed by acpmux for the shared session) + embed the SDK + its runtime in the agent image (NOTE: image currently has NO node/npm — claude-code is the native binary; a TS SDK shim needs a Node runtime, or use the Python SDK — decide at impl). For non-Anthropic models, the SDK can target the sidecar's /v1/messages Anthropic<->OpenAI converter (sp-9xr.15) via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, or use real Anthropic directly.

SEQUENCING (owner): do this AFTER the ACP enrichment (this epic's research-driven capability work) — once we know/decode the rich ACP capabilities (tool content/diffs, plans, todos, modes, fs/terminal), the shim can emit them and we get a high-fidelity Claude-on-ACP rather than a minimal-slice one. Depends on the enrichment children (to be filed from the research).

title: tmux/terminal-mode polish: opencode-tui sidecar config, status dot, claude key warning, UTF-8 locale
description:
Four bugs found running terminal-mode (tmux) spawns in the web UI + spawnctl attach:
(1) opencode-tui bypasses the sidecar: the dispatcher's opencode-tui case is 'exec spawn-tmux opencode' and SKIPS the opencode.json sidecar-provider config + OPENCODE_CONFIG + model=spawnery/<id> that the default opencode-served path generates, so the TUI uses opencode's built-in provider (zen) and autogenerates the session name. Fix: run the same config-gen (factor a shared shell fn) before launching the TUI so it goes through the sidecar.
(2) Web status dot wrong for tmux mode: openSession is gated off for tmux (App.tsx) and TerminalView's own socket has an empty onDown and never reports up, so ConnStatus.conn stays null -> dot hidden. Fix: TerminalView reports its socket state into useConnStatus (thread the callbacks down).
(3) Claude Code prints 'Detected a custom API key in your environment' because ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is in the env. Fix: provide the dummy key via an apiKeyHelper (settings.json) instead of the env var so no warning fires (verify live; fall back to customApiKeyResponses preseed if needed).
(4) Broken glyphs: tmux shows VT100 ACS line-drawing as q/x and claude ASCII-substitutes (●->_) in the web. Root cause: image has no UTF-8 locale (debian:stable, LANG unset=POSIX). Fix: ENV LANG=C.UTF-8 LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 (C.utf8 is available, no locales pkg needed); spawn-tmux 'tmux -u'; ExecPrefixFor forwards -e LANG/LC_ALL (both docker + crictl lanes). One root cause for both tmux q/x and web ●->_.
close_reason: 4 terminal-mode bugs fixed + verified live: (1) opencode-tui now runs the sidecar provider config (factored setup_opencode_provider() shared with opencode-served) so it no longer hits opencode zen; (2) web status dot driven by TerminalView reporting its socket state into useConnStatus (App->AppShell->TerminalView), incl. F1 guard against stray reconnecting() on intentional teardown; (3) claude 'custom API key in environment' warning removed by supplying the dummy key via apiKeyHelper (claude-settings.json) instead of ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env (verified no warning + TestCPClaudeTuiEndToEnd still passes via sidecar); (4) UTF-8 glyphs fixed (ENV LANG/LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 + tmux -u + ExecPrefixFor forwards LANG on docker lane; crictl relies on image ENV) — tmux/web render box+● not q/x/_. Reviewed APPROVED w/ minor; F1 fixed. e2e 7/7 PASS.

title: [demo-mvp] Web UI: URL history + document.title reflect the current view
description: Today the web SPA switches views via React useState only (web/src/App.tsx + shell/AppShell.tsx + Sidebar) — no router, no browser-history integration, and document.title is static. Result: Back/Forward don't work, views aren't deep-linkable/shareable/refresh-stable, and the tab title never reflects context. Make navigation update the URL + title as the user moves between app parts. Requirements: (1) push a history entry (history.pushState) for each navigable view — marketplace/templates list, app Detail (e.g. /app/<id>), spawns list, active spawn (e.g. /spawn/<id>), and the other top-level sections; (2) handle popstate so Back/Forward restore the corresponding view state (selected app/spawn, active tab); (3) on first load, parse the URL so a deep link / refresh lands on the right view; (4) set document.title per view (e.g. 'Spawnery — Templates', 'Spawnery — <app title>', 'Spawnery — <spawn name>'). Keep it lightweight — a tiny pushState/popstate helper is fine; a full router lib only if it pulls its weight. Don't break the live /ws/session handling (App.tsx uses location.host for the socket). Coordinate path/title naming with the Marketplace->Templates rename ([[sp-u5q]]) so URLs/titles use the final names. Update Playwright e2e (e2e/*.spec.ts) that assume in-place view switching to also assert URL + title where relevant.

title: Assess + decide: relax agent caps (CHOWN/SETUID/SETGID) so runtime apt works in spawns
description: The agent container runs CapDrop=ALL (manager.go DropAllCaps:true), so runtime 'apt update/install' inside a spawn fails: apt drops to the _apt user (setuid/setgid/setgroups) for its download sandbox and chowns its cache, all needing CAP_SETUID/CAP_SETGID/CAP_CHOWN. Today we bake tools into the image instead. TASK: assess the exact risk of granting back ONLY {CHOWN, SETUID, SETGID} to the agent and decide whether to enable it (likely dev-only opt-in env, prod stays CapDrop=ALL). Notes: (1) most defensible on the runsc/gVisor lane where gVisor sandboxes syscalls regardless of in-container caps; (2) no-cap workaround exists today: 'apt-get -o APT::Sandbox::User=root update' (update works; install postinst chowns may still fail); (3) risk surface: in-container setuid/chown could aid privilege games within the container, but container escape still needs a runtime/kernel/gVisor bug. Deliverable: risk write-up + decision (enable dev-only / keep hardened / sandbox=root-only). MVP-scope per owner.

title: CP: handle duplicate node-id registration (displace old stream + epoch-guard teardown)
description: registry.Add keys by node-id (r.m[id]=n), so a 2nd node registering the same id silently overwrites the registry entry while the 1st stream stays alive. Both streams' Heartbeat/reconcileInventory act on the shared id (the duplicate that doesn't host a spawn marks it UNREACHABLE), and EITHER stream's disconnect defer runs DropNode(id)+MarkUnreachable, killing routes for a spawn that's fine on the other. Symptom: spawn flips to UNREACHABLE shortly after create + web can't connect when two spawnlets share NODE_ID. FIX: on Register with an existing id, displace (close/cancel) the OLD connection and take over; guard the per-connection defer so it only Remove/DropNode when the registry entry is STILL this connection (epoch/token), so the displaced old's teardown doesn't nuke the new node's routes. Node-side fail-fast (terminal port bind) already added as a local guard for the single-host case.
close_reason: Implemented: registry per-connection token + lastBeat; Register rejects duplicate id when existing node is alive (heartbeat <15s), silently displaces a dead one; runNode teardown token-guarded (RemoveIfCurrent) + displaced streams stop (IsCurrent). Node-side fail-fast on terminal-port bind already added for the same-host case. Unit tests cover reject-alive/displace-dead/epoch-guard.

title: Research GPT Store (and peer stores) to verify Spawnery differentiation hypotheses
description:
Competitive/positioning research feeding the eval-harness design (sp-dkz §7) and demo messaging. Verify (confirm/refute, with sources) four hypotheses about why GPT Store apps are structurally weaker than Spawnery Apps:

H1 — NO take-home artifacts: GPT Store apps cannot produce a durable, user-owned, portable artifact store (no per-user data repo the app accumulates + user can export/clone). => LLM-wiki-style 'owned growing corpus' apps impossible. CAVEAT to check: Code Interpreter can emit one-off downloadable files in-session; confirm there is no PERSISTENT user-owned repo across sessions.

H2 — NO user/creator containers: apps = prompt + creator knowledge files + Actions (calls to an EXTERNAL API the creator hosts elsewhere) + OpenAI's own ephemeral Code Interpreter sandbox (runs the MODEL's generated python, not creator-provided container code). => zork-game-master / persistent-server-state apps not expressible as creator-controlled containerized code. CAVEAT: an Action could call the creator's own externally-hosted server — note this is 'you host it elsewhere', not the store running your container/OCI image.

H3 — bespoke UI creation: GPT Store GPTs are authored via the GPT Builder UI (conversational builder + configure form), NOT as a git repo you build with your own agent-coding tooling (Claude Code etc.) and publish. CAVEAT: OpenAI Assistants API is programmatic but is a SEPARATE product from the consumer GPT Store publish path — confirm you cannot push a repo-built artifact to the Store.

H4 — no evals in creator studio: the GPT Builder has only a manual live 'preview' chat; no automated scenario testing / simulated-user / scoring. Confirm.

For each: cite sources (current as of 2026) and map to Spawnery's contrasting capability (data repo / hardened container + ACP / repo-built apps / eval harness sp-dkz). Output feeds spec §7 positioning + demo-MVP §5 messaging rules. Peer-store spot-check (Poe/Coze/Character.AI) where cheap.

title: Eval harness MVP — creator-private 'Test Your App' (measurement-only slice of sp-kkk)
description:
MVP 'taste of orchestration': pull the MEASUREMENT-ONLY slice of sp-kkk forward. A first-party, operator-run harness drives an App-under-test (SUBJECT spawn) through a creator-authored scenario via a DRIVER/mock-human (ACP persona) and grades the resulting ACP transcript with a JUDGE (E8 scanner sp-ydz reused in rubric mode). Creator-private 'Test Your App' report (transcript + per-dimension scorecard). Three modes: Scenario, Replay (grade a recorded conversation, no mock-human), Diff (re-run scenarios across app@sha).

EXPLICIT NON-GOALS (MVP): no optimizer (-> sp-kkk phase 2); no native spawn-to-spawn comms / app-spawns-app (-> sp-0tw); no public quality badge / eval-gated trust tier (-> long-term arc, gated on BYOM for cross-family judging); no user-facing multi-agent.

KEY: orchestrator is operator-run (spawnctl-style over internal/acp), NOT a marketplace app, so it does NOT need sp-0tw and sidesteps the spawn-creation security crux. Depends only on the existing demo spawn engine.

Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-eval-harness-mvp-design.md. Backed by two deep-research passes (2026-06-03) + competitive verification (AgentCore/Braintrust/LangSmith). Per planning workflow: design only, no impl until all epic designs done + adversarial roast.

title: WS auto-reconnect via partysocket + escalating connect-timeout + reconnecting indicator
description: Replace the chat WebSocket's single new WebSocket() with a partysocket-backed self-reconnecting socket (web/src/shell/reconnectingSocket.ts) that retries forever with an escalating per-attempt connect-timeout (500/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, success resets to 500; via mutating partysocket _options.connectionTimeout on close). Re-run the ACP handshake on each (re)connect; teardown closes the socket; ledger poll still owns connect/teardown while partysocket owns WS-level reconnect. useConnStatus gains reconnecting (yellow) + disconnected (red after ~12s grace). Pin partysocket exact version + structural guard test. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-ws-reconnect-partysocket-design.md.
close_reason: WS auto-reconnect shipped & merged: ReconnectingSocket (partysocket@1.1.19 pinned) with escalating connect-timeout 500ms/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, handshake re-runs per (re)connect, useConnStatus reconnecting(yellow)/disconnected(red after grace), guard+behavior tests. (Re-closing: today's branch checkout/merge reverted the original close commit 4f1de17 via the .beads import hook.)

title: Background-spawn 'working' indicator in sidebar (per-spawn turn-state beyond the live WS)
description:

That MVP shows the 'working…' affordance only for the ACTIVE spawn, because turn-state is delivered over the single live WS (spawn/turn notification + spawn/history.turn). To show which BACKGROUND spawns are mid-turn (sidebar dots), turn-state must propagate beyond the one live connection.

Approaches to weigh (discuss before building):
- Fast-track 'busy' status propagation over WS.
- Retain WS connections when switching between spawns (at least for a while, with a timeout) so background turn-state keeps flowing.
- Full plumb-through: turn-state up through CP + DB (relates to sp-pc4 CP state/DAO) so listSpawns/ledger carries a per-spawn busy bit the sidebar polls.

Likely interacts with sp-r7t (node-owned session / reconnect) and sp-pc4 (CP state layer).

title: Node readiness probe: report ACTIVE only after the agent answers ACP initialize
description: Today the node reports ACTIVE the instant mgr.Create returns (containers launched), but goose takes ~5s more to answer ACP initialize — so the spawn shows green ~instantly while the WS can't connect for ~5s, defeating the sp-wi3 starting lifecycle. Fix: in attacher.startSpawn, after Create, open an attach and send an ACP initialize, waiting for the matching response (readiness probe) before reporting ACTIVE; timeout(30s)->mgr.Stop+ERROR. Lane-agnostic via mgr.Attach (Docker stdio / CRI adapter UDS), retrying the attach while it fails (CRI adapter startup). Repeated initialize verified safe vs goose (see sp-r7t, the spec-compliant follow-on). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-node-readiness-probe-design.md.
close_reason: Node readiness probe: startSpawn now sends an ACP initialize and awaits the response (probeReady, retry-attach to 30s for CRI adapter startup) before reporting ACTIVE; timeout->mgr.Stop+ERROR. So the UI's green dot means 'agent answered' not just 'container launched', and yellow/starting covers the ~5s goose boot. awaitInitialize unit-tested (6 cases); Manager.Stop made teardown cancellation-immune. 2 tasks two-stage reviewed; final whole-branch review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 11/11 clean (probe runs in the real node lane vs stub). Follow-ups: sp-r14 (probeReady retry-loop test). Merged to master.

title: Visible spawn 'starting' lifecycle: async CreateSpawn + yellow dot, WS gated on active, 'waiting' header state
description: Today CP CreateSpawn blocks until the spawn is ACTIVE, so the web never shows a 'starting' period. Make CreateSpawn async: validate sync, store.Create(starting), return id immediately, provision in a background goroutine that SetActive/SetError on the node signal (status already DB-recorded). Web: createSpawn returns starting -> add to list (yellow dot) + setActiveId + conn=waiting, do NOT openSession; poll ListSpawns (1s while any starting, else 3s) and openSession when the active spawn flips to active (green), errored() on error (red, stays in sidebar, removable via Stop). useConnStatus/ConnStatus gain a 'waiting' state (grey pulse). Sidebar starting dot = yellow. Chat input disabled until connected. CP tests: createActive + suspend/resume tests wait-for-active after async CreateSpawn (new waitActive helper). spawnctl uses standalone spawn.v1, unaffected. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-spawn-starting-lifecycle-design.md.
close_reason: Visible spawn starting lifecycle. CP CreateSpawn async (validate sync, store.Create starting, return id, background provisionSpawn -> SetActive/SetError on node signal, logs provision failures). Web: yellow starting dot, grey-pulse 'waiting' header, WS gated on active via poll (1s while starting else 3s) + pure nextConnAction policy, failed/unreachable spawns red+removable, chat input gated on connected. 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 10/10 (retries:2 absorbs ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED env flake). Merged to master.

title: Node-relay transcript recording + replay (history survives browser reload, both lanes)
description: The slice-2 spawn/history replay lives in the in-pod acpadapter, which is only in the CRI/runsc lane — so in the Docker/self-hosted lane (just dev) history is lost on browser reload (client buffer gone, no adapter in path). Move recording to the NODE relay (in both lanes path, outlives client reloads): extract the recorder to internal/transcript (shared with the adapter); node holds a long-lived per-spawn recorder registry, tees relay bytes, and replays spawn/history on each client (re)connect. Lane-aware (node records Docker lane only; CRI keeps the adapter) to avoid double-replay. Web onHistory/historyToItems unchanged. Goal: history survives reload in just dev, proven by an e2e test. Follows-on from sp-r6b/sp-02f; partial step toward sp-3nb (durable/cross-device history).
close_reason: Node-relay transcript recording + replay. Recorder extracted to internal/transcript (shared w/ adapter); node records each spawn's transcript at the relay + replays spawn/history on every client (re)connect, Docker lane only (lane-aware). History now survives a browser reload in just dev — proven by e2e (10/10, reload test wipes the client buffer so only node replay can restore). 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE.

title: Node should retry/reconnect to CP (not exit when CP is down)
description: Observed during just dev: if CP isn't up yet at node startup (or the CP drops), node.Run returns an error and cmd/spawnlet log.Fatals -> the node exits immediately. Fix: a reconnect loop with backoff so the node waits for the CP at startup and reconnects if the stream drops (re-Register; the CP already reconciles a returning node per the state-machine design). Only exit on ctx cancellation (clean shutdown). The Manager + its running spawns persist across reconnects (mgr is passed into node.Run; a fresh attacher re-registers).
close_reason: DONE merged a8343cd. node.Run refactored into a reconnect loop + per-connection runOnce: dials+Registers+heartbeats+receives under a per-connection connCtx (defer cancel stops heartbeat + relay sessions on disconnect, no goroutine leak); backoff 1s->30s exp (reset after >30s healthy); returns ONLY on ctx cancellation. cmd/spawnlet log.Fatal(node.Run) with ctx=Background now loops forever (reconnects) instead of dying when CP is down. Fixes the just-dev startup race + CP-restart resilience. mgr persists across reconnects (running spawns survive). Hermetic TestRunRetriesUntilContextCancelled (unreachable CP -> returns context err after retrying, not the conn err). First internal/node test (partial sp-sj8).

title: [epic] Adopt gVisor (runsc) for the cloud tier — Phase 2 isolation
description: Research verdict (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-gvisor-isolation-research-results.md): adopt gVisor on the cloud tier now, on top of the existing floor. Same isolation as Cloud Run/GKE Sandbox/Modal/Tencent for ~identical workload; closes the host-kernel + runc-escape CVE class (CVE-2019-5736, CVE-2024-21626, CVE-2020-14386, CVE-2022-0185) that runc+seccomp does not. Plumbing exists (CONTAINER_RUNTIME=runsc knob). Plan: PHASE 1 (pre-req, cheap) read-only rootfs + drop-all caps + user-namespace remap + MOVE egress floor to host veth (see floor-placement bug). PHASE 2 install runsc (systrap platform, directfs+overlay+netstack defaults) + canary cloud spawns 5%->25%->100% + per-image runc fallback w/ audit + CI compat gate. cgroup limits (sp-ach) STAY (gVisor doesn't self-enforce in-sandbox limits — pin sandbox to host cgroup). PHASE 3 (threshold-gated) microVM (Kata/Cloud Hypervisor) for a hard tier: regulated/enterprise/high-value tenants, GPU/eBPF/FUSE/io_uring needs, or /dev/kvm available. Run our own benchmark vs real agent traffic before sizing.
notes: BLOCKER found in local goose+sidecar runsc verification (sp-vaw, P0): the current Docker two-container shared-netns pod (agent --network=container:<sidecar>, reaches sidecar on 127.0.0.1:8080) is BROKEN under per-container gVisor — each container is its own Sentry/netstack, loopback not shared. Verified: goose ITSELF is gVisor-compatible (boots+ACP+prompt under runsc); host egress floor (sp-ff2), cap-drop (sp-s9u), and runsc preflight (sp-zb2) all work under runsc on the real pod. The ONLY break is inter-container loopback. So Phase-2 needs a pod-as-one-sandbox model (containerd CRI pod / k8s RuntimeClass) OR collapsing sidecar+agent into one container — NOT raw docker-run-per-container. Decide in sp-vaw before any runsc cloud rollout.

Wire into spawnlet manager ContainerSpec/HostConfig. Cheap, big blast-radius reduction even before runsc.
close_reason: Done, merged b9f5342: agent container --cap-drop=ALL (always) + gated read-only rootfs (HARDEN_ROOTFS, default off) with /tmp tmpfs. userns-remap = daemon-config doc (deployment.md/ISOLATION.md), not per-container code. Host-verified under runsc: cap-drop + ro-rootfs (blocks rootfs writes, /tmp writable). ro-rootfs default-off pending goose image validation.

title: Scheduler routing by node class + author-self-host rule for unverified apps
description: Use the CP-recorded node class (sp-2as) to place spawns. FLIPPED POLICY (user 2026-06-01): an UNVERIFIED app may run ONLY on a self-hosted node OWNED BY THE APP'S AUTHOR (creator_id == node owner). Rationale: unblocks the author's dev iteration with NO review and NO added risk (their own self-hosted node already has access to all the author's data; nothing new is exposed). scanned/reviewed apps route normally (cloud or any). title: sp-pc4 part 3: persist container node_id + compensating cleanup on post-Provision failure
description: From the part-2 CP-rewire review. Three related robustness gaps deferred to part 3 (the inventory/reconciliation protocol): (1) CreateSpawn orphan window — if SetActive fails AFTER Provision succeeded (route bound + node container live), CreateSpawn returns CodeInternal with NO cleanup; the container + route leak and the row is stuck 'starting' (boot sweep flips it unreachable but the container/route survive). Add compensating rt.Drop + StopOnNode + SetError on the post-Provision failure path. (2) SetError's error is swallowed (_ =) on Provision failure — at least log it so a stuck 'starting' row isn't silent. (3) The container's node_id is NEVER persisted: Create inserts NodeID='' and SetActive only flips phase; Provision returns the chosen node id but CreateSpawn discards it. So spawn_containers.node_id stays empty and LiveContainersByNode/Adopt (the part-3 reconcile machinery) cannot function until the CP persists the node binding (e.g. SetActive should also set the container's node_id, or add a bind step). All three are the same theme: record the node binding + run compensating cleanup on the post-Provision edge. Part of sp-pc4 part 3 (lifecycle RPCs + generation fencing + inventory reconciliation).
close_reason: Done in sp-pc4 part 3a (merged 0978c24): node_id persisted via SetActive + compensating cleanup on the post-Provision orphan window. (Was filed depending-on sp-pc4, but it's a completed sub-item; sp-pc4 stays open for part 3b.)

description: Replace the CP's ephemeral in-memory maps with a durable transactional state layer (Bun + modernc sqlite + Postgres + goose + :memory: tests), and implement the ENTIRE CP-side spawn lifecycle on it. spawns table = the CP index. Owns: store package (schema owners/apps/app_versions/spawns/spawn_mounts, full interface, Bun impl, hermetic tests); CP-side state machine (starting/active/suspending/suspended/error/deleted) incl. transitions, boot + node-evict reconciliation (->suspended), idempotent seeding; lifecycle RPCs CreateSpawn(rewired)/ListSpawns/SuspendSpawn/ResumeSpawn/DeleteSpawn + Session ownership-via-store + auto-resume-on-attach + single-session takeover; CP->node plumbing (new Suspend message; StartSpawn carries mount bindings). Node-side persist/restore is a STUB teardown here (data survives only once E3 persistent storage + sp-gd9 WIP-ref land). DB authoritative for ownership; router.Owner() removed. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-state-dao-layer-design.md.
design: Hard constraints: ergonomic cross-repo transactions; thin-ORM scanning (no manual row->struct); SQL stays visible (no query-hiding ORM magic); one codebase over two dialects; strong (not absolute) pref for pure-Go/cgo-free; mature/boring, no bleeding edge. SQLite/Postgres are SEED options only — research must challenge the engine choice.

title: Orphaned-container handling: labels + reap-on-startup + idle reaper
description: The spawnlet's SpawnStore is in-memory, so on spawnlet restart it forgets running pods while Docker keeps them alive -> orphans. Also no idle-timeout in the slice, so clients that never call StopSpawn leak pods. DEFERRED from the slice (user decision: track only). Plan when picked up: (1) label every container at create (spawnery.managed=true, spawnery.spawn-id=<id>); (2) reconcile-on-startup: list labeled containers and remove orphans (clean slate; in-mem store is empty post-restart); (3) background IDLE-REAPER goroutine that tears down spawns idle past a timeout -- this is the E1 idle-timeout feature and is the only part needing an always-running process. Pairs with E1 runtime core (sp-ei4). For now: manual StopSpawn + docker rm for stragglers during dev.
notes: PROGRESS (2026-06-05): items 2+3+4 of the REMAINING list landed (TDD; node+spawnlet suites green, golangci-lint clean). Item 1 (CP inventory consumption) was already done via sp-pc4 reconcileInventory. (2) GENERATION FENCING on Stop: node now ignores a Stop whose generation is older than the live spawn (attach.go staleGen + Manager.SpawnGeneration); a current/0 gen tears down as before. Suspend half deferred -> the fence helper is ready but Suspend message handling itself is sp-gd9 (still an inert TODO in handle's default case). (3) IDLE-REAPER: Pump tracks lastActivity (refreshed by any relay frame in either direction) + attached(); attacher.reapIdle two-stage budgets (detached idleDetachedTimeout=15m, attached idleAttachedTimeout=60m) reaps via stopSpawn; idleReapLoop wired into runOnce (ticks every 1m, tied to connCtx). LOSSY teardown (reports STOPPED) — lossless suspend-on-idle is sp-gd9, gated on E3. (4) GRACEFUL SIGTERM TEARDOWN: Manager.StopAll(ctx); cmd/spawnlet uses signal.NotifyContext + gracefulStopAll after node.Run returns (fixes the sp-8mn e2e container leak). New tests: TestStopAllTearsDownEverySpawn, TestStopFencesStaleGeneration, TestPumpTracksActivityAndAttached, TestReapIdleTwoStage. NOT YET COMMITTED. All 3 title items (labels + reap-on-startup + idle reaper) now done -> issue is closeable, handing the Suspend-message fence + lossless idle-suspend to sp-gd9.
close_reason: Orphan-container handling complete: labels + reap-on-startup (prior, 57c7aba) + idle reaper (this session). Items 2/3/4 of the remaining list landed TDD: generation fencing on Stop (node ignores stale-gen), two-stage idle reaper (15m detached/60m attached, lossy STOPPED), graceful SIGTERM teardown (Manager.StopAll wired via signal.NotifyContext; fixes sp-8mn e2e leak). Item 1 was already done via sp-pc4 reconcileInventory. Suite green, golangci-lint clean. Handed to sp-gd9: lossless suspend-on-idle (E3-gated) + the Suspend-message fence (helper ready).

title: ROAST: cold-start-every-wake = bad UX for coaches; no latency budget
description: Found by 3/4 (arch #7, product F9, ops #5). Realistic first-token on resume = 5-15s warm-image/small-repo, 30s-2min+ cold-image/large-repo/busy-GPU (image pull + pod create + git clone /data + agent boot + skill load + key agreement + model first token). Coaches' whole value is continuity, but every 'checking in' pays full cold-start + full-thread reload — and the loyal daily user with a big thread gets the WORST latency + re-billed replay tokens. No doc owns this number. FIX: pull warm-pool/keep-alive + node repo-cache (resume=fetch not clone) + pre-pulled images + incremental/summarized thread load into MVP for coaching apps; loading-state UX in E6; set a first-token-on-resume acceptance metric. Affects E1 §3/§4, E6 §3, E7.
notes: RESOLVED (user): accept cold-start startup times for MVP; warm-pool/repo-cache/incremental-load DECLINED. Key correction: cold start = NEW session, NO conversation-thread replay. Continuity = agent reads structured files in /data at session start (not transcript-into-context). Kills the 'loyal user worst latency' + 're-bill replay' concerns. Tune idle timeout to match LLM provider prompt-cache TTL (~5min) since cache expires regardless. DOC FIX NEEDED: E1 §3 / E7 §4 / E6 §3 currently say resume 'reloads the prior thread to restore context' -- correct to 'fresh session; continuity via file reads in /data'.

description: Permission/consent enforcement; egress allowlist enforcement; audit pipeline (sidecar conversation-content logging for Spawnery-operated cloud only); abuse signals; flagging/takedown for the open registry. Design doc section 9.
notes: Design doc (v1): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-spawnery-e8-trust-safety-audit-design.md. Decisions: audit = full content on Spawnery-operated infra, encrypted, ~30d TTL auto-purge, break-glass access, disclosed (self-host exempt, BYO-on-cloud audited); review = scanner-first (LLM-as-judge over manifest+persona+skills) human-on-flag; enforcement = tiered (live block severe-only + async delist/suspend leaving user data untouched + appeal); inline classifier shares infra with review scanner (real ML build item); free-tier content-blind abuse signals. title: acpdial shim + nori terminal attach (spawnctl attach to acp-mode spawn -> shared session)
description: Tiny 'acpdial' Go shim (inverse of acpexec): pipe stdin/stdout <-> a TCP dial to 127.0.0.1:7000. Configure nori's custom agent (LocalDistribution command in ~/.nori config, or -c override) to launch acpdial so nori joins acpmux's shared goose session WITHOUT a nori fork. Wire spawnctl attach for acp-mode spawns: internal/spawnlet/terminal.go terminalInnerCmd acp branch -> launch nori (driving acpdial); add Manager argv helper mirroring TmuxAttachArgv; dispatcher 'nori' case if needed. Requires Spawn.Mode reaching spawnctl (check it is in the attach path). v1 = shared view + serialized prompts, NO arbitration / NO read-only roles (one user across devices). e2e: TWO ACP clients (node-pump web frame path + an acpdial/2nd client) see the SAME goose turn; spawnctl attach to an acp spawn launches nori joining the LIVE conversation. Depends on acpmux. See [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]].
close_reason: spawnctl attach to acp-mode spawn launches nori joining the SHARED goose session. acpdial stdio<->TCP shim (nori's custom 'spawnery' agent -> acpmux:7000); nori config baked ([[agents]] name+slug + [agents.distribution.local].command=/usr/local/bin/acpdial). terminalInnerCmd acp branch -> nori argv (spawnctl unchanged). Found+fixed load-bearing bug: nori uses STRING JSON-RPC ids; acp.Message.ID *int silently dropped nori's initialize -> introduced RawID (verbatim string|int round-trip; int helpers for self-assigned ids) across acp/pump/acpmux/ocadapter/stubagent. Reviewed APPROVED (RawID contained to agent-facing ACP wire; web Frame protocol untouched). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpNoriAttach PASS — nori rendered real 'banana' via nori->acpdial->acpmux->goose AND a 2nd client late-joined+replayed nori's turn (shared session). Full e2e regression green.

title: acpmux: in-container single-session ACP multiplexer (relocate the fanout pump)
description: Standalone Go binary 'acpmux' that becomes the ONE ACP client of 'goose acp' (spawns it over stdio, owns a single shared session) and presents ACP-agent semantics on local TCP :7000 (where acpexec->goose listens today), multiplexing N ACP clients onto that one session: eager single goose session; each connecting client's initialize/session-new is mapped/joined to the shared session with frame-log REPLAY for late joiners; session/update notifications fan out to all clients; session/prompt from any client routes to the one session (serialized); permission requests broadcast, first response wins. Port the logic from internal/node/pump.go. Replace the dispatcher goose-acp case (acpexec goose acp -> acpmux goose acp); acpmux accepts MULTIPLE connections (acpexec was one-at-a-time). MINIMAL node change: the node pump dials :7000 as before and sees acpmux transparently as the agent (it is the first client; session created; web ChatView keeps working). Verify the existing TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd still passes through acpmux. RISK: node reconnect/late-join must rejoin the existing session + replay (not spawn a 2nd goose) — handle in acpmux. See [[acp-shared-attach-architecture]].
close_reason: acpmux: in-container single-session ACP multiplexer delivered. Spawns 'goose acp' (one shared upstream session S), presents canonical ACP on :7000 multiplexing N downstream clients (replaces acpexec for goose-acp); replay-log+cursor for late joiners, notification fanout, serialized prompts (exact per-client result correlation), permission broadcast first-wins. Ported from internal/node/pump.go; node pump unchanged + transparent. Review found+fixed 1 blocking ordering race (turn-end result racing session/update chunks) via single-writer ordered log (TestFanout 800/800), + 4 minors (prompt-error forwarding, Stop closes conns, id-0 sentinel, perm-ordering documented cosmetic). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd (transparent through acpmux) + TestAcpmuxTwoClientsShareSession (fanout+late-join replay, real goose) PASS.

title: Hermetic test for node probeReady attach-retry + deadline budget
description: probeReady (internal/node/ready.go) retries mgr.Attach (~250ms) until a deadline, then hands the REMAINING time to awaitInitialize. The retry loop + deadline-budget arithmetic (incl. attach eating most of the 30s, leaving a tiny/near-zero timeout) is currently untested — awaitInitialize is well covered, but probeReady's loop is exercised only by the e2e happy path (Docker attach succeeds first try) and the host CRI lane (adapter-startup retry). Add a hermetic test by introducing a seam: extract the loop into a helper taking an attach closure (func() (*runtime.AttachedStream, error)), then table-test fail-N-times-then-succeed + deadline-exhausted. Raised in sp-39u code review (Minor #3, non-blocking).

title: ACP-compliant reconnect: filter redundant initialize/session-new at the node relay
description: Our model is non-standard for ACP: one long-lived agent process (goose) with MANY client sessions attaching over its lifetime, so the web client re-sends 'initialize' (+ 'session/new') on every reconnect (switch-away/back, page reload). ACP agents are normally one-process-per-connection with a single initialize. To be spec-compliant + robust across agents, the node relay should own a single ACP session per spawn: do initialize+session/new ONCE, and filter/short-circuit the client's redundant initialize/session-new on reconnect (replaying the cached responses + rewriting sessionId), forwarding only session/prompt. Bonus: this gives real agent-memory across reconnect (same session) instead of today's visual-only node replay. Spun out of the spawn-readiness discussion (sp-wi3 follow-on); relates to the node-readiness-probe work which also relies on goose tolerating re-initialize.
notes: turn-state/queueing (sp-95v) shifted the disconnect-mid-turn stuck-state surface: the broker (internal/transcript.Recorder) now stays busy=true with an inflight id that no response will ever match if the client disconnects mid-turn. The sp-r7t fix (node owns the session, filters redundant initialize/session-new on reconnect) should also reset broker turn-state on re-initialize OR have the node emit an explicit spawn/turn{state:idle} on session re-open.
close_reason: Superseded by the long-lived per-spawn pump (sp-bjd, merged). The pump owns the single goose ACP session for the spawn's whole life and issues 'initialize'+'session/new' exactly once; thin clients only send prompt frames and never re-handshake. The redundant-initialize/session-new-on-reconnect problem this bead targeted no longer exists — there is no per-connection relay re-doing the ACP handshake to filter. (CRI lane brought to the same model in sp-j5b.)

Keep data-testid="status" (and add data-status for the light/e2e). title: MarkBootUnreachable doesn't sweep 'suspending' — CP crash mid-suspend strands the spawn
description: In SuspendSpawn (internal/cp/lifecycle.go), if the CP crashes after SetSuspending but before SetSuspended, the spawn is stranded in 'suspending': the in-handler SetError compensation is bypassed, and store.MarkBootUnreachable only sweeps {Starting, Active}, so boot reconcile won't recover it. Narrow window (a couple synchronous store calls; container already torn down). Fix: add 'suspending' to MarkBootUnreachable's status set (or a dedicated boot sweep that errors/recreates stranded suspending spawns). Found in sp-zer final whole-branch review. Epic sp-k20.

title: EPIC: tmux attach to a running spawn (raw terminal access)
description: EPIC (Phase 2): spawnctl 'tmux' attach into a running opencode spawn, concurrent with the web client, via opencode's shared server-authoritative session. mosh-server on the node; child execs into the spawn container (docker/CRI exec) running 'tmux new-session -A' -> 'opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096'. Control plane via CP (owner-only authz, node discovery, auto-resume if suspended); data plane is mosh UDP direct to the node (around CP for now; future: tunnel through CP<->node link for airgapped/NAT). Supersedes the earlier raw-PTY-channel framing: with opencode the 'terminal' is the opencode TUI sharing the same session, so no second relay channel is needed. See spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-05-opencode-swap-and-terminal-design.md.
design: Terminal runs INSIDE the container (node execs in); image ships opencode+tmux. Idle: only opencode-observable activity counts (terminal keystrokes bypass the adapter/SSE, so a silent TUI can be idle-suspended — accepted). Audit/E8: opencode TUI can run POST /session/:id/shell + edit tools = un-audited mutation path; accepted owner-only risk, disclosed. Dual permission answering: last-write-wins at the opencode server.
acceptance_criteria: GOAL: 'spawnctl tmux <spawn>' attaches a TUI to a running opencode spawn AND the tmux TUI and the web client see each other's inputs (shared opencode session). Attaching to a suspended spawn auto-resumes first. A second attach reattaches the existing tmux session. Owner-only enforced at CP.
notes:
SHARED-VISIBILITY PROVEN (2026-06-05) at the opencode/adapter layer: a 2nd client (simulating the in-tmux TUI) POSTed a prompt directly to the shared opencode session; the WEB side (node via adapter) saw it as ACP agent_message_chunk 'TUISIDE' through the adapter's SSE->ACP fan-out. Reverse direction is opencode's native broadcast to any SSE subscriber (the TUI). So the conceptual core of the goal is de-risked; Phase 2 is now PURELY the terminal transport plumbing: node mosh-server + exec-PTY into the container running 'tmux new -A -> opencode attach 127.0.0.1:4096' (sp-wsu.1), CP control+authz+auto-resume (sp-wsu.2), spawnctl tmux (sp-wsu.3). Node<->image TCP path (sp-5h3.4) already validated on real docker.
IMPLEMENTED + validated (standalone): spawnctl tmux -spawn <id> -> node /terminal -> mosh-server + exec tmux+opencode-attach on the shared session. Every automatable link validated incl. the in-container TUI rendering the web's prompts (shared visibility both directions). Only the final interactive mosh-client->PTY render needs a human TTY (inherent). CP-routed control plane is the remaining productionization (sp-wsu.2).

title: Conversation continuity across suspend/resume
description: Deferred from the spawn-lifecycle design (data-only resume for now). Persist the conversation transcript to the data repo per spawn.yml's conversation-history pointer on suspend; reload/replay on resume so a resumed spawn continues the prior chat. Gated per-app (some apps are stateless-per-session). Builds on the spawn lifecycle (sp); see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-spawn-lifecycle-design.md sec 5/10.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native spawn-to-spawn comms framework
description:
Enabling substrate for multi-spawn orchestration. Today a spawn drives ONE agent over ACP via the CP; there is no native primitive for an app/spawn to message or create sibling/child spawns ('app-spawns-app'). This epic adds a native inter-spawn communication + orchestration primitive: an orchestrator spawn that can create and drive child spawns, and/or spawn<->spawn messaging, built on the existing CP router/scheduler + transparent ACP relay.

WHY: prerequisite for native eval orchestration, sub-agent spawning, and agent-team orchestration. It is the 'communication engine' those tools build on.

SECURITY CRUX (hard requirement): spawn-spawning is sensitive under the open-marketplace trust model — open third-party apps must NOT get unscoped spawn-creation. The capability must be permissioned via the manifest permissions/consent/egress model and trust tiers (a scanner miss must stay contained). Quotas/concurrency caps from sp-ach apply.

SCOPE NOTE: post-MVP (after the demo's safety floor + marketplace). The demo's spawn engine (CP mediation slice, merged) is the foundation. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: POST-MVP EPIC: Native eval orchestration (one spawn evals/optimizes another)
description:
Eval-first LLM-app-building tooling, implemented as multi-spawn orchestration on the native comms framework. Roles, each a spawn/agent over the same communication engine: SUBJECT (app under test, possibly weaker/cheaper model) + DRIVER/user-simulator (feeds inputs / a fixed eval set) + JUDGE/OPTIMIZER (stronger model). Loop: run subject on the eval set -> judge scores against a rubric -> optimizer proposes prompt/instruction edits -> re-eval -> keep if better; promote best app@sha and/or per-model prompt.

WHY IT FITS: (1) makes the 'model half' thesis concrete — per-app, per-model scores answer 'which AI is good enough for which app', and the optimizer can auto-tune an app's prompt per model. (2) Marketplace CAPABILITY signal complementing the safety trust-tiers (e.g. 'scores 8.2/10 with DeepSeek') — could become CI-for-prompts in the publish flow. (3) Dogfoods multi-spawn orchestration. (4) Immediate internal use: discharges 'verify seed apps run on DeepSeek' (sp-iui).

START NARROW: measurement-only harness first (run app over eval set vs a model, judge-score vs rubric, report per-model scores; reuse internal/acp like spawnctl) — NO optimizer. Add the optimize loop as phase 2 once the eval signal is trusted.

DESIGN CRUXES: (a) eval dataset/rubric is the real artifact — creator-authored; shapes the manifest. (b) LLM-as-judge is noisy/biased/gameable — needs rubric + held-out set + mitigations (reference-guided, pairwise, juries) to avoid reward-hacking the judge. (c) keep optimization surface tiny: {prompt variants} x {models} before tools/data. (d) cost & nondeterminism -> offline/power-creator feature, competes with live users on the single demo box. (e) app-or-infra: prototype as an app/external harness (needs the spawn-comms capability), graduate useful parts into publish/CI.

PRIOR ART (survey before building): DSPy (MIPROv2/Bootstrap), OPRO, APE, EvoPrompt, TextGrad for optimization; promptfoo/Braintrust/LangSmith/Inspect/OpenAI-Evals for harnesses; LLM-as-judge bias literature. A deep-research prompt was drafted (2026-05-31 session) to map SOTA + gap analysis. Novel angle = eval/optimize loop as native multi-spawn orchestration on an isolated, model-agnostic, versioned-app substrate feeding a marketplace.

DEPENDS ON: the native spawn-to-spawn comms framework epic. Capture only; design later via brainstorming.

title: Local-app data sync + ACP client (Obsidian/IDE) for spawns
description:
VISION: let users view/edit a spawn's data in a native local app (Obsidian for the wiki; an IDE for code) while the agent works it remotely, and chat with the agent from the same app via an ACP-client plugin. The killer combo is **llm-wiki + an Obsidian ACP-client plugin**: the vault shows the wiki growing in real time while the user chats in the same window.

TWO SYNC LEVELS:
- (start) READ-ONLY local mirror, continuously updated from the remote. Sidesteps bidirectional sync; edits flow through the agent (chat). For git-backed mounts this is nearly FREE (user clones their own data repo + a tiny daemon git-pulls on push). Trap: Obsidian IS an editor, so users will edit the mirror and silently lose changes on the next pull -> must be enforced read-only or upgraded to bidir.
- (target, RICHER) BIDIRECTIONAL real-time sync: user and agent collaborate LIVE on the same files -> the agent researches/writes the bulk while the user views/corrects, and both see each other's edits in real time. This is the powerful version. Hard part = real-time bidirectional sync + conflict resolution (CRDT/OT per-file e.g. Yjs for text, or fast file-granular last-write-wins). PRIOR ART: Obsidian's livesync community plugins (self-hosted CouchDB/CRDT) are a precedent for exactly this shape. Overlaps with E3's DEFERRED bidirectional sync (sp-u53).

TWO DEPLOYMENT MODES (from the ACP-client research):
- REMOTE: mirror/sync for display + ACP chat with TRIMMED fs capability (agent stays self-contained on container data); two channels (sync + ACP). A network/stdio bridge is required (all current ACP clients are stdio + local-assuming).
- SELF-HOST: mount the user's local folder straight into the spawn as the data mount, keep ACP fs capability INTACT, REWRITE PATHS (vault path <-> /app/data). No sync needed -> bidirectional by nature (same folder).

RELATED / STRATEGIC:
- We will build our OWN ACP client (existing ones are IDE-shaped: assume local checkout, want an IDE). Study Zed + VS Code ACP-client impls for the client-half rendering (session/update, tool-calls, permission prompts), drop the IDE scaffolding.
- This is arguably the answer to the wiki RETENTION problem (sp-54f) WITHOUT building proactivity (sp-37x): the wiki lives in a tool you already open daily. And it makes 'your data is yours' visceral.
- ACP-client research (network bridge required; fs trim for remote; local-mount+path-rewrite for self-host) was confirmed via deep research; bridge idea = spawnctl refactored into a transparent stdio<->Session pipe + minimal initialize capability handshake.

OUT OF SCOPE for now; parked as a future direction.
notes:
SYNC OPTIONS (a menu, none decided):
1) git pull/push loop — already have it; commit-granular, coarse.
2) CouchDB/PouchDB replication (Obsidian Self-hosted LiveSync shape) — OFF-THE-SHELF, near-real-time SAVE-granular w/ MVCC conflict-merge. Arch: CouchDB hub <-> user's Obsidian (reuse the existing LiveSync plugin) <-> a spawn-side /app/data<->CouchDB replicator (the only piece WE build); one DB per spawn. NOT a character-CRDT. Downside: needs a hosted CouchDB. = ONE OPTION.
3) CRDT keystroke-realtime (Yjs/Automerge) — true simultaneous editing, conflict-free auto-merge. DESIRED to explore eventually.

PREFERENCE (user): AVOID hosting an external DB just for sync. Favor PEER-TO-PEER: the user's local app and the spawn synchronize DIRECTLY. Fits us well — the existing client<->spawn channel (per-session E2E / direct / relay) could carry the sync updates, no central DB. Prior art for P2P: LiveSync has a P2P/WebRTC mode; Yjs (y-webrtc) + Automerge are natively P2P. IDEAL TARGET = CRDT replicas on both peers (local app + spawn's /app/data), synced over the client<->spawn channel, keystroke-realtime, no hosted DB. Note the data-substrate tension (git = durable/portable/clean-exit vs CRDT/live-store = realtime collab); likely git as source-of-truth + a realtime layer reconciling to it.

title: Mountpoint creation for data mounts (avoid per-app .gitkeep)
description: runc can't create the nested mount destination /app/<path> inside an already-read-only /app, so the mount dir must pre-exist in the bind source. Slice workaround: each app ships an empty mount dir (examples/secret-app/data/.gitkeep). Cleaner options: (a) node mkdirs appPath/<mt.Path> before the ro bind (host dir is writable; mutates the app source dir); (b) when /app is assembled per-spawn from App@sha (full design), assembly creates the mountpoints. Decide + implement when /app assembly lands. Found during the per-mount-backends e2e (mounting rw /app/data onto ro /app failed until data/ existed in the source).

title: spawnlet: image auto-pull in StartContainer/manager (spec §8 step 3)
description: runtime.Docker.StartContainer assumes the image exists locally; spec §8 step 3 said 'ensure agent base image present -- pull if missing'. Not implemented (images built locally per README). Add cli.ImagePull on not-found before create, or in Manager.Create. Final-review finding. Acceptable for the slice; needed once images come from a registry (CP integration).

title: spawnlet: thread SidecarPort to the agent's OPENAI_HOST (or drop the knob)
description: ManagerConfig.SidecarPort is configurable, but deploy/agent/entrypoint.sh + the Goose OPENAI_HOST hardcode 127.0.0.1:8080. If SidecarPort != 8080 the agent silently can't reach inference. Fix: either drop the SidecarPort knob (slice always uses 8080) or thread the port into the agent container's OPENAI_HOST env in Manager.Create. Final-review finding (whole-slice). Harmless today (nothing overrides default).

title: [web] Nav-section exhaustiveness + dedupe templates-surface grouping
description: Follow-up from sp-jpn review (non-blocking quality). (1) AppShell.topView, TemplatesView.navToTab, and Sidebar's NAV table all use a default/fallthrough, so adding a new Nav section won't fail at compile time — add an exhaustive switch / satisfies / assertNever guard so a new section is caught by tsc. (2) The 'templates-surface sections' grouping ({templates,app,my-apps,publish}) is duplicated across those same three spots — extract a single shared predicate/constant in web/src/nav/nav.ts and consume it in all three. Files: web/src/nav/nav.ts, web/src/shell/AppShell.tsx, web/src/shell/Sidebar.tsx, web/src/views/TemplatesView.tsx.

title: Address SA1019 deprecations excluded from golangci-lint config
description: The sp-ooo golangci-lint config excludes SA1019 (deprecation) to stay bug-focused and green. Track + migrate the real deprecations it would have flagged: (1) cmd/cp/main.go + cmd/spawnlet/main.go use h2c.NewHandler (golang.org/x/net/http2/h2c, deprecated) -> set http.Server.Protocols for unencrypted HTTP/2 instead. (2) internal/cp/store: bun.In is deprecated -> bun.List/bun.Tuple (3 sites: reconcile.go, spawns.go x2). (3) internal/runtime/docker.go: NetworkSettings.DefaultNetworkSettings.IPAddress deprecated (use NetworkSettings.Networks) + client.IsErrNotFound deprecated (use cerrdefs.IsNotFound). Low priority; re-enable -SA1019 in .golangci.yml once cleared.

title: Post-demo: durable/true spawn history (agent memory across reconnect + cross-device + survives suspend)
description: Beyond the visual adapter-replay: true agent memory on reconnect (ACP session/load if goose supports) and/or CP/store transcript persistence that survives suspend and works cross-device. Gated alongside E3 persistent storage. From epic sp-k20 spec §Post-demo.

title: Post-demo: on-demand history pagination (remove acpadapter 500-item cap)

title: web marketplace UI polish nits (sp-znq follow-ups)
description: Optional polish from sp-znq final review (all non-blocking): (1) App.tsx clientRef.current not nulled on re-spawn teardown (unreachable via UI since busy disables input; tidiness). (2) Detail.tsx renders createdAt as raw epoch string (int64 Connect-JSON) — format as a date. (3) Publish.tsx no client-side required-field validation (relies on CP InvalidArgument toast; a pre-submit guard saves a round-trip). (4) Publish mount rows keyed by array index (harmless given append/filter-only).

title: DeleteSpawn/StopSpawn ignore auth ok-bool (owner, _ :=) — return Unauthenticated for consistency
description: Minor consistency nit from the part-3a final review. DeleteSpawn + StopSpawn use 'owner, _ := auth.OwnerFromContext' (ignore the ok bool) and delegate ownership to stop()'s store check; ListSpawns/CreateSpawn check ok and return CodeUnauthenticated. Safe in prod (the auth.Interceptor rejects unauthenticated before the handler), but if stop() is ever called from a non-HTTP path the empty owner yields a misleading PermissionDenied. Low priority: align DeleteSpawn+StopSpawn to check ok -> Unauthenticated. Also (minor) add an explicit store-layer TestMarkDeletedFromStarting.
